Title: (Jr.) Operations Systems Analyst (m/f/d)
Description:
About HOLY


HOLY is the soft drink revolution: With our powdered drinks HOLY Energy®, HOLY Hydration®, HOLY Iced Tea®, and HOLY Syrup Bottle® you get amazing taste across 50+ flavors while maintaining a good conscience—for your body & the environment.
Over 2M loyal customers: Started in a shared apartment by three best friends, we have scaled to Europe's fastest growing consumer brand—and our cult-like community, the HOLY Squad, loves it: 4.6 stars on reviews.io speak for themselves.
From D2C to Global Omnichannel: In less than four years, we've profitably scaled HOLY to more than €180M D2C revenue in DACH, FR, and the UK. Now scaling our retail business, we're on an exciting transition to becoming a true omnichannel rocketship.
We are still just getting started: Backed by the world's best consumer investors, we aim to revolutionize the soft drink industry and build a generational love brand: tastier, healthier & more sustainable. Our team of 170+ people aims high (Coca-Cola, we're coming for you!).


About The Position

The Operations Systems Analyst is the guardian of HOLY's data backbone — ensuring our ERP operates as the single source of truth that supply chain, finance, and analytics teams depend on every day. This role sits at the intersection of operations, data, and systems: investigating discrepancies, enforcing data standards, and building automated checks that prevent issues before they reach downstream teams.

As HOLY scales, this person will evolve from reactive troubleshooting into proactive data governance, making them a critical enabler of operational reliability across the business.

Responsibilities:


Data Integrity Ownership & Discrepancy Resolution: Lead the investigation and resolution of data discrepancies across Odoo ERP, WMS, and PIM — whether originating from user input, API syncs, or EDI integrations. Ensure that inventory quantities, product records, and transactional data remain consistent so operations, supply chain, and finance teams can execute on numbers they trust.
Master Data Standards & User Enablement: Enforce and maintain data entry standards across all ERP users — catching miscoded products, incorrect quantities, and improperly configured records before they cascade into reporting errors or blocked integrations. Onboard new users to correct data practices and drive targeted corrections when standards are breached.
Cross-System Reconciliation & Monitoring: Build and own regular reconciliation checks between Odoo, WMS, and PIM, developing automated monitoring where possible to proactively surface mismatches on inventory, shipments, and product data. Monitor API and EDI integration flows for sync failures or data mismatches and coordinate resolution with the relevant technical teams.
Pattern Recognition & Systemic Improvement: Track and document recurring data issues and system errors, identifying underlying patterns that signal root-cause problems rather than one-off incidents. Collaborate with technical and operational stakeholders to fix systemic causes and reduce the volume of repeated reactive fixes.
Process Design, Adherence & AI-Driven Automation: Document operational processes clearly, identify where they're being bypassed or degraded, and drive the behavioral and system changes needed to close gaps. Use AI tools to build automated adherence checks and monitoring solutions that scale process compliance without manual overhead.


About You


ERP & data fluency: You have 2–4 years of hands-on experience working with ERP systems (Odoo strongly preferred; NetSuite or Business Central also relevant) and understand how supply chain, finance, and analytics teams interdependencies
Detail-obsessed: You catch discrepancies others miss — whether it's a mismatched inventory quantity, an API response that doesn't add up, or a product record configured just slightly wrong.
Structured diagnostic thinker: You approach problems systematically — separating symptoms from root causes, documenting findings clearly, and knowing when to escalate versus resolve independently. You'd rather fix the pattern than patch the same issue twice.
Technically self-sufficient: You can navigate ERP, WMS, and PIM systems independently — using logs, documentation, hands-on testing, and AI tools to get to root cause without waiting for someone to point you in the right direction. SQL proficiency is a strong plus.
Clear cross-functional communicator: You're comfortable working with procurement, logistics, and accounting teams — translating technical findings into language that non-technical stakeholders can act on, and driving resolution without unnecessary back-and-forth.
AI & automation mindset: You actively look for opportunities to replace manual monitoring and repeated fixes with automated checks and AI-assisted workflows. You see tooling as leverage, not a nice-to-have.


Our Offering

In today's job market, A-players have many options to choose from. As part of our offer, we prioritise providing a productive working environment and opportunities for growth. In addition to competitive compensation, including company shares, you can expect:


A successful and growing company: Join a company that has been growing profitable and quickly for the last 36+ months and have raised a series B, while many other e-commerce companies are struggling.
Huge impact and career growth: Enjoy huge impact and hence explosive career growth, build your own market, and shape HOLY’s internationalisation in a top market
Awesome team: Join an awesome team of colleagues who are ambitious, funny, down-to-earth, and authentic. Get to know us and see for yourself!
Top product: Marketing is important, but a good product is essential. Our differentiated soft drinks have won over customers. Check out our Trustpilot reviews or contact us for free samples.
Future of e-commerce: Be part of the future of e-commerce and help set new standards in many areas. Work with the best minds in the industry.
Startup culture: Join a startup culture that values flat hierarchies, fast pace, and personal development. With us, you can make a real impact.
Perfect setup: Enjoy a perfect setup that includes all the requirements of a modern employer, such as a central office in Berlin Prenzlauerberg, flexible working hours, and much more.
How we support your growth: flexible hours, 28 vacation days, 30 work-from-abroad days, €1,500 learning budget, Urban Sports Club membership, 63€ public transport subsidy, and biannual 360° feedback tied to your compensation.


Find out more about HOLY and our team on our careers page.

The Process

As we are experiencing rapid growth and have ambitious plans, the optimal start date for the position is today. Nonetheless, we are prepared to patiently await the arrival of the ideal candidate, recognizing that quality ultimately yields the best results.


Getting to know you: Eric, our Talent Acquisition Manager will have a 30 minute screening interview where we will talk to you more about HOLY and the position and see if our expectations match.
Hiring Manager interview: Sofiia, our Sr. Ops Project Manager will dive a bit deeper into your experience and details of the role with you.
Case study & on-site interview: As soon as you have successfully completed the first interviews, you will receive a case study. In a second meeting at our office, the team will discuss your case study with you and answer questions about our workspace, the HOLY Temple.
Reference calls: At this stage, we’d like to connect with 1–2 former colleagues or managers who have worked closely with you. These conversations help us better understand your working style and strengths from those who know you professionally.
Interview with Frederick: As a strong cultural fit is important to us, you will have a final meeting with Frederick, one of our HOLY Founders. This gives both you and us the opportunity for a final check.
Job offer and decision: If everything goes well, you will receive an offer and we are ready to embark you this journey together with you!


Typically his process should take around 2 weeks.

Any questions or concerns?

If you have any questions or feedback, please send an email to jobs@weareholy.com.

About Us

HOLY Softdrinks GmbH

10405, Berlin

Title: Werkstudent (m/w/d) Power BI & Sales Performance Analytics
Description:
Werkstudent (m/w/d) Power BI & Sales Performance Analytics

Berlin, Hamburg-Bahrenfeld
ab sofort
Teilzeit
befristet



Über uns

Elis ist Europas führender Experte für Textildienstleistungen. Mehr als 60.000 Menschen sorgen an 480+ Standorten in 31 Ländern dafür, dass die textilen Versorgungskonzepte von ca. 400.000 Kunden weltweit laufen wie am Schnürchen. Eine Glanzleistung unserer Mitarbeitenden, die unsere Hochachtung verdient!

In Deutschland und Österreich sorgen unsere 49 Standorte dafür, dass Kunden aus Hotellerie und Gastronomie, dem Gesundheitswesen sowie Industrie und Handel zuverlässig mit Miettextilien versorgt werden. Das Headoffice in Hamburg unterstützt die Niederlassungen mit zentralen Services und sorgt für reibungslose Abläufe im Hintergrund. So entstehen beste Voraussetzungen für den täglichen Betrieb in unterschiedlichsten Branchen. Dieses Konzept ist nicht nur wirtschaftlich sinnvoll, sondern auch nachhaltig – und befindet sich auf einem kontinuierlichen Wachstumskurs. Wir suchen dich als

Deine Aufgaben
Power BI: Aufbau, Pflege und Weiterentwicklung unserer Dashboards zu Sales Performance, Lead‑Management sowie KPI‑ und Forecast‑Tracking
Datenmanagement: Anbindung, Bereinigung und Strukturierung relevanter Daten aus CRM (Microsoft Dynamics), Marketing‑ und Partner‑Quellen sowie regionalen Excel‑ und CSV‑Dateien
Management‑Reporting: Erstellung aussagekräftiger Entscheidungsgrundlagen für Monatssteuerungen, Reviews, Kick-offs und Forecast‑Runden
Automatisierung: Weiterentwicklung unseres Reportings hin zu stabilen, automatisierten BI‑Views und Reduktion manueller Excel‑Prozesse



Dein Profil
Studium: Laufendes Studium, z. B. in Wirtschaftsinformatik, Data Science, BWL mit Analytics‑Schwerpunkt oder einem vergleichbaren Bereich
Zahlenverständnis: Sehr gutes Verständnis für Zahlen und Freude daran, komplexe Sachverhalte in klare, saubere Modelle zu überführen
Power BI: Praxiserfahrung in Power BI, insbesondere in Datenmodellen, Measures und Visuals
Excel: Gute Kenntnisse in Excel, idealerweise inkl. PowerQuery und strukturierter Datenaufbereitung
Datenkompetenz: Erste Erfahrung mit relationalen Daten oder CRM‑Strukturen von Vorteil
Arbeitsweise: Strukturierte, eigenständige Herangehensweise – du arbeitest nicht nach einem festen Pflichtenheft, sondern an echten Fragestellungen
Sprachen: Deutsch auf Muttersprach‑ bzw. sehr gutem Niveau, Englisch sicher in Wort und Schrift



Wir bieten
Corporate Benefits:

Mitarbeiterrabatte für verschiedene Aktionen & Marken

Perfekte Vereinbarkeit:

Unsere flexiblen Arbeitszeiten ermöglichen es, Studium und Job unter einen Hut zu bekommen und die Einsatztage so zu legen, wie es der Vorlesungsplan erlaubt

Homeoffice:

An bis zu 6 Tagen im Monat bequem von zu Hause aus arbeiten

Modernste Arbeitsausstattung:

Sowohl im Büro als auch im Homeoffice können die besten Bedingungen genossen werden

Teamwork:

Gelebtes Teamwork mit flachen Hierarchien sowie agile Arbeits- und Entscheidungsmethoden

Echter Support:

Die Führungskräfte und Profis vor Ort sind auch nach der Einarbeitung jederzeit da – für die persönliche und fachliche Weiterentwicklung gibt’s dazu noch diverse Maßnahmen on top

Onboarding:

Ein strukturiertes und auf die Position abgestimmtes Einarbeitungsprogramm




Schnell und einfach bewerben

Uns interessieren Menschen mehr als aufwendige Motivationsschreiben oder astreine Werdegänge. Bewirb dich jetzt online über den „Bewerben“-Button.

Neugierig geworden? Mehr Infos zu Elis und wie es sich anfühlt, zu uns zu gehören, gibt es auf unserer Unternehmenswebseite oder auf unseren Social-Media-Kanälen. Ein Blick hinter die Kulissen gibt es auf Instagram und Facebook unter @elis.karriere.

Deine Ansprechperson:

Kai Bönsch
Recruiting Specialist
Tel: +49 40 3562-52237

Elis Group Services GmbH
Daimlerstraße 73
22761 Hamburg

Title: Data Science & Business Analytics, praxisnah lernen, 100 % Remote (Weiterbildung)
Description:
So wirst du Teil der Zukunft: Datenkompetenz leicht gemacht

Wir sind die DataSmart Point Academy, ein spezialisierter Weiterbildungsträger mit Fokus auf digitale Zukunftskompetenzen.

Unser Ziel: Menschen praxisnah für Data, Analytics und Künstliche Intelligenz qualifizieren – flexibel, modern und anerkannt.

Title: Werkstudent*in Data & AI in Commercial Effectiveness
Description:
Für unseren Standort im Herzen von BERLIN (Nähe Park Gleisdreieck) suchen wir ab dem nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt Unterstützung (15-20h/Woche) im Team Commercial Effectiveness.

Über uns

Als Werkstudent im Bereich CFC Commercial Effectiveness unterstützt du unser Team dabei, Vertriebsstrategien zu optimieren, Kundensegmentierungen weiterzuentwickeln und datenbasierte Entscheidungen zu fördern. Du arbeitest direkt an praxisrelevanten Projekten – mit einem besonderen Fokus auf datengetriebene Analysen– und erhältst tiefe Einblicke in die kommerzielle Exzellenz eines globalen Pharmaunternehmens.

Dein Profil


Du bist immatrikuliert in einem Studium wie z. B.: BWL, Business Administration, Wirtschaftsinformatik, Gesundheitsökonomie oder ähnliches.
Du sprichst Deutsch auf C1/C2 Niveau und bringst gute Englischkenntnisse (B2 oder höher) mit.
Du denkst analytisch und gehst strukturiert an Aufgaben heran.
Du arbeitest eigenständig und eignest dir schnell neue Themen an.
Du arbeitest gerne im Team und kommunizierst klar und auf den Punkt.
Du arbeitest zuverlässig und mit einem hohen Qualitätsanspruch.
Du kannst komplexe Inhalte einfach und verständlich erklären.


Folgende Interessen Oder Kenntnisse Bringst Du Mit

Datenanalyse & Tools


Sehr gute Kenntnisse in MS Office (insb. Excel, PowerPoint, PowerQuery)
Erste Erfahrung mit BI-Tools (z. B. Power BI, Tableau)
Grundkenntnisse in SQL
Erste Erfahrung mit Datenbanken (z. B. Snowflake) von Vorteil
Interesse an pharmazeutischen Märkten und Vertriebsprozessen


Künstliche Intelligenz (AI)


Interesse an Large Language Models (LLMs) im Business-Kontext
Erfahrung mit Tools wie ChatGPT, Copilot oder Claude
Grundkenntnisse in Prompt Engineering und AI Use Cases


Prozessautomatisierung


Erste Erfahrung mit Microsoft Power Automate oder ähnlichen Tools von Vorteil
Verständnis für die Automatisierung von Geschäftsprozessen


Deine Aufgaben

Datenanalyse & Reporting


Analyse von Vertriebs- und Marktdaten sowie Datenextraktion mittels SQL
Erstellung von Reports und Dashboards zur Steuerung der Commercial Effectiveness
Identifikation von Trends und Ableitung konkreter Handlungsempfehlungen


AI & LLM-Anwendungen


Evaluierung und Einsatz von LLM-basierten Tools (z. B. ChatGPT, Copilot, Claude) für Business Use Cases
Unterstützung bei der Entwicklung von Prompt-Strategien und AI-Anwendungsfällen im Vertrieb
Mitarbeit an Prototypen und Proof-of-Concepts für datengetriebene Lösungen


Prozessautomatisierung


Unterstützung bei der Automatisierung von Geschäftsprozessen mit Microsoft Power Automate
Identifikation von Automatisierungspotenzialen und Entwicklung von Workflows


Commercial & Sales Effectiveness


Analyse und Optimierung von Vertriebsprozessen sowie KPI-Auswertungen
Unterstützung bei Territory-Alignment-Projekten
Erstellung von Präsentationen und Entscheidungsvorlagen


Wir bieten dir


Flexible Arbeitszeiten und die Möglichkeit zum teilweisen mobilen Arbeiten
Ein modernes, internationales Arbeitsumfeld
Zugang zu Sanofi-Lernplattformen und internen Trainings
Eine individuelle und strukturierte Einarbeitung in Deine Aufgaben sowie Mitarbeit an strategisch relevanten Projekten


Wir freuen uns auf die Bewerbung. Bitte richte diese ausschließlich elektronisch an unseren Partner Provadis Professionals GmbH unter Werkstudent*in Data & AI in Commercial Effectiveness

Title: Business Analyst, Data & AI (m/f/d)
Description:
Company Description

Power the energy transition with ib vogt GmbH

Founded in 2002, ib vogt GmbH is a leading global platform for utility‑scale solar development. We deliver turnkey PV plants and battery storage projects worldwide, covering development, design, financing, EPC, and operations, while continuously expanding our IPP portfolio. With more than 3 GW of commissioned capacity, our international teams operate across 30+ countries, turning ambitious ideas into clean energy solutions that power communities and accelerate the global energy transition.

Job Description

As Business Analyst in Data & AI, you act as the bridge between business functions and digital delivery. You work embedded across business units to identify high-value problems and translate them into clear technical requirements for engineering teams.

You combine strong business analysis and stakeholder management skills with technical affinity, using AI tools to prototype solutions, understand data and code at a working level, and shape ideas before development. You work closely with engineers, Digital PMO, and business stakeholders, reporting to the Head of Data & AI.

Key Responsibilities

Requirements Discovery & Translation


Run workshops and stakeholder interviews to understand business needs and translate them into clear requirements (user stories, acceptance criteria, process flows, data models)
Maintain and prioritize a structured backlog (e.g. Azure DevOps), ensuring continuous alignment with evolving business needs and validating delivered solutions through testing and sign-off


Business Partnering


Act as embedded partner to business units (Project Development, EPC, O&M, Corporate Functions)
Build strong stakeholder relationships and proactively shape demand for Data & AI solutions as the key liaison between business and technical teams


Solution Shaping & Prototyping


Use AI and low-code tools (e.g., Copilot, Power Platform, Azure AI) to build rapid prototypes and proof of concepts
Translate business needs into feasible solution designs in collaboration with engineers and support early feasibility assessment


Delivery & Coordination


Coordinate delivery of initiatives, including scope, timelines, dependencies, and risks
Support prioritization with Digital PMO and communicate progress to technical and non-technical stakeholders


Adoption & Change


Support rollout, training, and user adoption of delivered solutions
Collect feedback and feed it into backlog and roadmap while ensuring responsible AI usage.


Qualifications

Required Qualifications & Experience


Degree in Business, IT, Engineering, or related field (or equivalent experience)
4+ years’ experience as Business Analyst / Product Analyst / similar bridge role
Experience working with both business stakeholders and technical teams


Skills & Competencies


Strong English communication skills, able to translate between business and technical audiences
Advanced user of AI tools for analysis, drafting, and prototyping
Understanding of AI capabilities/limitations to assess feasibility and set expectations
Ability to build quick low-code prototypes and read SQL/basic code
Good understanding of data, systems, APIs, and integrations
Strong requirements engineering skills (user stories, acceptance criteria, process/data flows)
Structured, proactive, and able to manage multiple stakeholders and initiatives
Collaborative mindset with ability to balance business needs and delivery reality


Preferred


Experience with Microsoft ecosystem (Power Platform, Power BI, Azure, Copilot tools)
Experience in the renewable energy sector is a strong plus
Certifications: IIBA (ECBA/CCBA/CBAP), Scrum/SAFe, Azure or AI fundamentals (AZ-900, AI-900).


Additional Information


A truly international working environment with colleagues from all over the world
An open-minded, friendly, dynamic, and highly motivated team
Interesting and challenging tasks, and thus a scope of professional growth and development
We encourage both team work and personal responsibility
Competitive remuneration and motivating benefits.


At ib vogt, we are committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and respectful workplace where everyone can thrive. We believe that embracing diverse perspectives drives innovation and helps us better serve the global communities we operate in. As an equal opportunity employer, we provide employment opportunities without regard to age, ethnicity, religion or belief, gender identity, sex, sexual orientation, disability, pregnancy or maternity, marriage and civil partnership, or any other protected status.

We strive to create an accessible recruitment process for all candidates and are happy to discuss reasonable accommodations to support your application. As a global company, we acknowledge that accessibility provisions may vary by location depending on local infrastructure and legal requirements. If you have a disability or require any adjustments during the recruitment process, please let us know - we will do our best to support you.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
About CuspAI

CuspAI is the frontier AI company on a mission to solve the breakthrough materials needed to power human progress. While nature took billions of years to perfect molecules, we are harnessing AI to unlock trillion-dollar materials breakthroughs in months, not millennia. Our founding team is the most cited in the world, comprised of world-class researchers in AI, chemistry and engineering.

We are working on some of the hardest and most important challenges including energy, clean water, the future of compute, and carbon capture, and this is just the start of what our 'search engine' for next-generation materials will unlock.

We invite you to be part of a diverse, innovative team at the intersection of AI and materials science, working to create impactful partnerships that drive innovation, scalability, and industry collaboration. This work matters. Your work matters.

We’re on the cusp of the on-demand materials era. Join us.

The Role

As we grow, we are seeking a Data Engineer to play a crucial part in driving our research and development efforts forward.

Your Impact

As a Data Engineer you will be part of the new team building the infrastructure that underpins and acts as the critical bridge between raw chemical data and our machine learning models.

Your main focus will be to build the pipeline infrastructure and tooling for data ingestion, moving towards self-serve setup for the scientific team members. You'll also be responsible for securing, collecting, cleaning, standardising, and tagging diverse chemical datasets to create high-quality training data for our ML researchers while working closely with our chemistry team to ensure scientific accuracy.

What You Will Do

Data Pipeline Development


Design and build robust data pipelines for materials science datasets, experimental results, and computational chemistry outputs.
Develop processes to integrate diverse data sources including materials databases, literature, patent filings, and laboratory instruments.
Create automated workflows for processing crystallographic data, molecular structures, and materials properties (you don’t need to have direct domain experience - we can help bring you up to speed!).
Build scalable systems to handle high-throughput computational chemistry calculations and experimental data.


Data Quality & Standardisation


Partner closely with the scientific and research teams to implement automated quality checks for crystal structure data, chemical compositions, and experimental measurements.
Create standardisation protocols for materials nomenclature, units, and measurement conditions.
Build monitoring systems to ensure data integrity across all pipelines.


Collaboration & Integration


You will also be working hand in hand with ML researchers to understand data requirements for model training and inference.
Partner with materials scientists to ensure accurate representation of domain knowledge in data schemas.
Integrate with laboratory automation systems and computational chemistry software.
Support real-time data needs for AI-driven materials discovery experiments.


Must Have Skills and Qualifications


You are someone who gets excited about the opportunity to enable scientists to work on world changing challenges in this domain, with a personal interest in the potential applications of the technology that Cusp is building.
You’re a builder of tools and infrastructure who enjoys making life as easy as possible for the teams, providing self-serve, reliable and scalable ingestion pipelines.
You have at least 3+ years experience in data engineering roles, preferably in scientific or research environments - you would be joining as a data engineering subject matter expert who can not only work autonomously but also provide guidance on best practice.
High level of proficiency in Python and databases with experience in large-scale data processing - as part of our engineering team you’ll be programming regularly, not just scripting.
You’re an advanced user of workflow orchestration tools (e.g. Airflow, Prefect, Dagster, Flyte or similar).
Solid experience with containerisation (Docker, Kubernetes) and CI/CD practices.
You have direct experience handling large/complex datasets and are interested in working with scientific packages.
You’re a fast learner when it comes to new tools/systems.
You enjoy (and have experience in) designing systems that scale with growing data volumes and user demands.
Understanding and appreciation of DevOps practices is also important.


Bonus Points (But Not Critical)


You’ve worked with data from scientific computing (simulations or experiments).
Knowledge of machine learning data requirements and MLOps practices, including pre-processing/processing as part of model training.
An academic background in Materials Science, Chemistry, Chemical Engineering, or related field.


Even more bonus points if you have an understanding of crystallography, materials properties, and computational chemistry concepts!

What we offer


A competitive salary: We value and reward impact and growth
Equity in CuspAI: You have a stake in the success of the company
Time off to stay fresh: 28 days holiday (DE, NL, UK) or 21 days holiday (JP, SG, US), in addition to local public holidays
‘Gold Standard’ parental leave: 26 weeks (primary caregiver) and 12 weeks (secondary caregiver) at full pay - we look after you and your family while we work on the most important materials discovery problems together
Professional development budget: We invest in your career development so you can stay up to date with the latest industry knowledge or add to your skills to increase impact and growth
Solve meaningful problems: See how your work has a direct impact on advancing materials science and solving sustainability and climate-related problems through the creation and application of bleeding-edge SOTA technology and revolutionary techniques
True interdisciplinary teamwork: Be part of a deeply collaborative environment bridging AI research, computational chemistry, and experimental science - work with world-class researchers and engineers who enjoy sharing knowledge and supporting each other


Join us in shaping the future of materials with AI. Together, we can create groundbreaking solutions for a more sustainable world.

CuspAI is an equal opportunities employer committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace. We do not discriminate on the basis of sex, race, religion or belief, ethnic or national origin, disability, age, citizenship, marital, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity, pregnancy or related condition (including breastfeeding), veteran status, or any other basis protected by applicable law.

We actively encourage applications from all backgrounds and value the unique perspectives and contributions that diversity brings to our team.

Please let us know If you require any specific adjustments during or after the interview process. We will do everything we can within reason to accommodate.

  • You’re a builder of tools and infrastructure who enjoys making life as easy as possible for the teams, providing self-serve, reliable and scalable ingestion pipelines.
  • You have at least 3+ years experience in data engineering roles, preferably in scientific or research environments - you would be joining as a data engineering subject matter expert who can not only work autonomously but also provide guidance on best practice.
  • High level of proficiency in Python and databases with experience in large-scale data processing - as part of our engineering team you’ll be programming regularly, not just scripting.
  • You’re an advanced user of workflow orchestration tools (e.g. Airflow, Prefect, Dagster, Flyte or similar).
  • Solid experience with containerisation (Docker, Kubernetes) and CI/CD practices.
  • You have direct experience handling large/complex datasets and are interested in working with scientific packages.
  • You’re a fast learner when it comes to new tools/systems.
  • You enjoy (and have experience in) designing systems that scale with growing data volumes and user demands.
  • Understanding and appreciation of DevOps practices is also important.

Bonus Points (But Not Critical)

  • You’ve worked with data from scientific computing (simulations or experiments).
  • Knowledge of machine learning data requirements and MLOps practices, including pre-processing/processing as part of model training.
  • An academic background in Materials Science, Chemistry, Chemical Engineering, or related field.

Even more bonus points if you have an understanding of crystallography, materials properties, and computational chemistry concepts!

What we offer

  • A competitive salary: We value and reward impact and growth
  • Equity in CuspAI: You have a stake in the success of the company
  • Time off to stay fresh: 28 days holiday (DE, NL, UK) or 21 days holiday (JP, SG, US), in addition to local public holidays
  • ‘Gold Standard’ parental leave: 26 weeks (primary caregiver) and 12 weeks (secondary caregiver) at full pay - we look after you and your family while we work on the most important materials discovery problems together
  • Professional development budget: We invest in your career development so you can stay up to date with the latest industry knowledge or add to your skills to increase impact and growth
  • Solve meaningful problems: See how your work has a direct impact on advancing materials science and solving sustainability and climate-related problems through the creation and application of bleeding-edge SOTA technology and revolutionary techniques
  • True interdisciplinary teamwork: Be part of a deeply collaborative environment bridging AI research, computational chemistry, and experimental science - work with world-class researchers and engineers who enjoy sharing knowledge and supporting each other

Join us in shaping the future of materials with AI. Together, we can create groundbreaking solutions for a more sustainable world.

CuspAI is an equal opportunities employer committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace. We do not discriminate on the basis of sex, race, religion or belief, ethnic or national origin, disability, age, citizenship, marital, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity, pregnancy or related condition (including breastfeeding), veteran status, or any other basis protected by applicable law.

We actively encourage applications from all backgrounds and value the unique perspectives and contributions that diversity brings to our team.

Please let us know If you require any specific adjustments during or after the interview process. We will do everything we can within reason to accommodate.

Title: Werkstudent Data Analyst - Reporting Services / Data Engineering & Intelligence (w/m/d)
Description:
Begleite KPMG bei den zukünftigen Herausforderungen unserer Kund:innen. Begeistere auch Du Dich für die Vielfalt unserer Fragestellungen - und mach gemeinsam mit uns den Unterschied. Die KPMG IT Service GmbH ist die IT-Beratung und -Dienstleistung der KPMG AG Wirtschaftsprüfungsgesellschaft. Du verantwortest gemeinsam mit Deinem Team die IT-Infrastruktur und unterstützt die Fachabteilungen bei der Bereitstellung von effizienz- und effektivitätssteigernder Business Software.

Deine Aufgaben

Du denkst nicht in Problemen, sondern gehst lösungsorientiert jede neue Herausforderung an? Dann kannst Du Dich hier einbringen:



Du unterstützt unser Team bei der Entwicklung und Weiterentwicklung moderner Reporting- und Analytics Lösungen im Unternehmensumfeld.
Du analysierst und strukturierst große Datenmengen und bereitest diese adressatengerecht auf.
Du wirkst aktiv in Projekten rund um Microsoft Fabric mit.
Du erstellst aussagekräftige Dashboards und Reports in Microsoft Power BI.
Du unterstützt bei der Weiterentwicklung und Migration bestehender Alteryx Workflows.
Du entwickelst kleinere Datenpipelines und Automatisierungen, z. B. mit Python.
Du führst Qualitätssicherungen und Tests von Reports sowie Datenmodellen durch.



Dein Profil



Du befindest Dich in einem laufenden Studium der (Wirtschafts-) Informatik, Data Science oder eines vergleichbaren Studiengangs.
Du bringst erste praktische Erfahrungen im Bereich Datenanalyse, Reporting oder Business Intelligence mit.
Idealerweise hast Du bereits Berührungspunkte mit Power BI, Python, SQL oder Alteryx.
Du interessierst Dich für moderne Data Plattformen und Technologien wie Microsoft Fabric.
Du verfügst über ein ausgeprägtes analytisches Denkvermögen sowie eine strukturierte und eigenständige Arbeitsweise.
Du arbeitest gerne im Team und überzeugst durch eine hohe Lernbereitschaft.



Deine Benefits



Willkommen im Team Damit Du von Beginn an voll bei uns durchstarten kannst, begleiten wir Dich mit strukturierten Onboarding-Maßnahmen während der gesamten Orientierungsphase. Dabei steht Dir immer eine persönliche Ansprechperson zur Seite.
Flexibel & erfolgreich Bei uns kannst Du Studium und erste Praxiserfahrung bequem vereinbaren. Dabei erhältst Du intensive Einblicke in die Arbeitswelt bei KPMG. In Absprache mit Deinem Team kannst Du Deine Arbeitszeit flexibel gestalten. So kommt Dein Studium nicht zu kurz.
Feedback & Entwicklung Vom ersten Tag an bist Du fester Bestandteil des Teams und übernimmst Verantwortung in herausfordernden (Teil-) Projekten. Mit regelmäßigem Feedback fördern wir zudem Deine persönliche und fachliche Weiterentwicklung.
Wertvolles Karrierenetzwerk Bei unseren YOUnior .Network-Aktivitäten stehen Vernetzung und der gemeinsame Austausch zwischen Auszubildenden, Dual Studierenden, Praktikanten/Praktikantinnen und Werkstudenten/Werkstudentinnen im Fokus.
Die abgebildeten Leistungen können je nach Position und Standort leicht variieren.

Title: Werkstudent (m/w/d) Data & Analytics
Description:
Deine Mission

Wir verändern eine ganze Branche – verändere sie gemeinsam mit uns!

You made my day – mit unserer innovativen und marktumwälzenden Plattform geben wir bereits einer Vielzahl von Menschen weltweit Zugang zu einer neuen, flexiblen und selbstbestimmten Arbeitswelt und bieten unseren Kunden durch unsere Software gleichzeitig einen komplett virtuellen und marktrevolutionierenden Kundenservice.

Sichere unser weiteres Wachstum und mach mit uns den Unterschied als Werkstudent (m/w/d) Data & Analytics im Hauptstadtoffice in Berlin im hybriden Modell.


Präsentation & Moderation: Du erstellst Präsentationen für unsere Data & Analytics Round Tables, um richtungsweisende Entscheidungen gemeinsam treffen zu können
Berichte & Analysen: Du unterstützt bei der Erstellung detaillierter Berichte und tiefgehender Analysen, die unsere Data-Strategy vorantreiben
Datenqualität sicherstellen: Du trägst zur Verbesserung und Überwachung der Datenqualität bei und identifizierst Optimierungspotenziale
Stetiges Lernen: Du erweiterst kontinuierlich dein Wissen und hältst dich über aktuelle Entwicklungen in Data & Analytics auf dem Laufenden
Enge Zusammenarbeit: Du arbeitest eng mit dem Head of Data & Analytics und Expertenteams zusammen, um innovative Lösungen zu entwickeln


Dein Skillset


Studium & Fachkenntnisse: Du bist Student:in in einem relevanten Fach wie Computer Science, Mathematik, Naturwissenschaften oder Kommunikationswissenschaften, besitzt bereits erste Berufserfahrung und bist etwa 20 Stunden die Woche verfügbar
Hands-on Mentalität & Kommunikationsstärke: Du bist praktisch veranlagt, kundenorientiert und präsentierst Informationen klar und überzeugend in deutscher und englischer Sprache
Technisches Verständnis & Daten-Expertise: Du verfügst über Erfahrung im Datenbankdesign, SQL, Python und Statistik
Analytische Fähigkeiten & Data Science Know-how: Du bist kreativ und flexibel in der Analyse komplexer Probleme und kennst dich mit Algorithmen aus
Methodische Skills: Du besitzt erste Erfahrung mit Tools wie Tableau sowie Workflow-Tools und starke Projektmanagementfähigkeiten sowie eine gesunde Portion Humor runden dein Profil ab


Dein Winning Deal


Deine Benefits: Neben weekly Pizza und Snacks vor Ort, bieten wir dir außerdem einen Joblunch-Zuschuss (4 x 6,90 € im Monat)t, eine bezuschusste Wellpass-Mitgliedschaft, Rabatte via "Corporate Benefits" sowie grandiose Team- und Companyevents.
Dein Arbeitsplatz: Bei uns arbeitest du im flexiblen, hybriden Modell aus einem unserer hundefreundlichen und modernen Offices
Dein Footprint: Eine Position in einer profitablen Company, in der du die Freiheit und das Vertrauen erhältst, diese mitzugestalten und mit uns gemeinsam an der “future of work” zu arbeiten.
Die Werte: Wir punkten mit einer offenen, authentischen Kultur und einem leistungsstarken sowie humorvollen Team, das sich schon jetzt darauf freut, mit dir mutige Entscheidungen zu treffen und neue Wege zu gehen, die uns zu nachhaltigen Erfolgen führen.
Deine Chance: Bei uns hast du vom ersten Tag an die Möglichkeit, Verantwortung zu übernehmen, Innovationen voranzutreiben und gemeinsam mit einem Team aus Expert:innen das Unternehmen und dich weiterzuentwickeln.


Über uns

Du suchst ein profitables Scale-Up, welches dir langfristige Perspektiven sowie ein disruptives Business-Modell mit einer wachstumsorientierten Unternehmenskultur bietet? Außerdem möchtest du über die gewohnten Standards hinauswachsen, bringst Eigeninitiative mit und schätzt es, Themen aktiv mitzugestalten?

Dann bist du bei yoummday genau richtig!

yoummday: “You made my day” – Als innovative SaaS Plattform verbinden wir Unternehmen mit hochqualifizierten Freelance-Callcenter-Agent:innen (“Talents”) weltweit. Unsere digitale Lösung ermöglicht flexible, skalierbare und effiziente Kundenbetreuung – ganz ohne klassische Callcenter-Strukturen. Wir sind ein familiengeführtes Münchener Scale-Up mit über 350 Mitarbeitenden (“Daymaker”) an 5 Standorten in Europa und 1 in den USA – weiteres Wachstum ist vorprogrammiert ;).

Werde Teil unseres Teams und setze mit uns neue Maßstäbe im AI-gestützen Kundenservice, um smarte, datengetriebene Lösungen zu erschaffen, die begeistern.

Wir bei yoummday setzen auf Vielfalt und Chancengleichheit. Wir begrüßen Bewerbungen unabhängig von Geschlecht, Herkunft, Alter, sexueller Orientierung, Religion oder Behinderung. Unterschiedliche Perspektiven machen uns stärker – deshalb fördern wir aktiv ein inklusives Arbeitsumfeld, in dem alle ihr volles Potenzial entfalten können.

Title: AI Data Engineer
Description:
About Qorelo

Qorelo uses AI to automate some of the most mission critical work in the enterprise: the management of SAP systems. SAP runs the core of the world's largest companies, including finance, supply chain, and operations, yet keeping it modern is slow, manual, and consultant heavy. We're changing that.

We're moving at lightning speed to revolutionize how enterprises run their IT stack, starting with SAP, the heart of many enterprises. Without a strong base system, innovation can never truly succeed. Our




AI agents help teams integrate, migrate, and modernize SAP faster and with fewer errors, compressing work that used to take months. We've raised $3.5M from HPI Ventures, the fund tied to the institute of SAP co-founder Hasso Plattner, along with many other great backers. We already work with Fortune 500 and DAX 40 companies, including Mercedes-Benz.




Our promise

Our team is made up of true innovators, builders, and entrepreneurs. What we offer is an environment to grow, with real ownership of not just your day to day work but the direction of the company itself. We're looking for people who want to shape a company, not just join one.




What You'll Own

Design and build the data layer that maps relationships across SAP projects: configurations, outcomes, domain patterns
Own our metadata architecture, including how data is labelled, linked, versioned, and made queryable
Build pipelines that ingest SAP telemetry and consulting learnings and output clean, structured knowledge
Work with the AI team to feed the right signals into the models
Improve the system with every customer and every deployment




What You Need

Strong data engineering background across pipelines, schema design, and data modelling
Experience with knowledge graphs, graph databases, or relational modelling of complex domain data
Comfortable with large, messy, semi structured data from enterprise systems
Systems level thinker who designs architecture first, not just query by query
High standards for data quality




Nice to Have

Familiarity with SAP data models or ERP data structures
Experience with embedding pipelines, vector stores, or LLM data infrastructure

Title: Senior Data Analyst (Relocate to Cyprus)
Description:
We are looking for an experienced Data Analyst to join our Player Care team at Scorewarrior. The Player Care team is at the forefront of providing customer service to our players, as well as safeguarding the community integrity through enforcing Fair & Safe Play, improving moderation efforts, and ensuring operational excellence while at it.




Scorewarrior is a game developer and publisher based in Limassol, Cyprus. The company focuses on the 4X strategy genre, with an ambitious goal to grow its hit game, Total Battle, to become the #1 strategy game in the world, and this role is key to achieving this. Our team consists of 200+ passionate, talented, self-driven, and continually developing professionals who love making high-quality games.




Our product: We are currently working on Total Battle — an MMO 4X strategy game with a deep political system and lots of player interaction. Players take on the role of a commander and need to develop their city, forge alliances, fight against monsters, and conquer other players. We're constantly developing new game mechanics, and the project is continuing to grow.




At the heart of our Player Care department, our mission is to excite our players with exceptional customer care, ensuring they feel valued and supported throughout their journey with us.




What you’ll do

Build and maintain the single "Source of Truth" semantic data layer for the entire Player Care department, enabling non-technical leads to perform ad-hoc analysis.
Design, build, and maintain reliable metrics dashboards tailored to the operational needs of different Support and Trust & Safety teams.
Ensure all operational metrics (CSAT, SLA, etc.) are clearly defined, reliable, and calculated correctly across all reports.
Assist and mentor the support team with SQL query composition and data literacy.
Conduct A/B tests to measure the effect of Support and Trust & Safety initiatives on relevant departmental and broader business metrics.
Analyze the correlation between support quality, bug-related friction, and subsequent player behavior, with a strong focus on VIP cohorts.
Partner with the safety team to analyze data for fraud detection, including social engineering and account sharing.
Perform time-series forecasting for workload volume based on the LiveOps calendar to optimize shift headcount and SLA coverage.
As a future ambition, build and maintain an early warning predictive model for VIP player churn.




What you have

Strong SQL skills and data modeling experience
Experience with BI tools (e.g. Tableau or equivalents)
Proven experience in product or operational analytics
Ability to design analytical events and metrics aligned with product goals
Strong analytical thinking and ability to translate data into actionable insights
Confident knowledge of mathematical statistics and probability theory
Hands-on experience with causal inference and A/B testing
Patience and willingness to advocate for data literacy and help operational teams learn basic data concepts




Nice to haves

Hands-on experience with ClickHouse and dbt
Experience analyzing LiveOps features, complex game economies, or VIP player behavior, ideally in mid-core or 4x strategy games.
Experience working with Customer Support or Trust & Safety (fraud detection, toxicity)
Experience working with large player bases and complex event-driven systems




Where you’ll work

We value face-to-face communication, and we strongly believe that genuine human interactions move us closer to our ambitious goal. This is part of our culture, and this is why this job offers no remote or hybrid work opportunities.

You will be working in Limassol, Cyprus. If you’re not from around here, fear not! Our dedicated relocation team will provide comprehensive support helping you and your family move to Limassol.




What we offer

The best team in the world!
Salary of 5000-7000 eur net/month + 13th salary.
Fully-covered relocation to Limassol, Cyprus for you and your family.
A modern and sunny office space close to the sea.
Private health insurance for you and your family members.
A monthly school allowance for your children at pre-school and school-age.
Lunch compensation.
Personal development opportunities paid for by the company: professional and language courses, books, etc.
Bonuses for outstanding results, years worked, getting married, and the birth of a child.
Monthly office parties and epic, annual offsite company birthday and Christmas parties.

  • Ensure all operational metrics (CSAT, SLA, etc.) are clearly defined, reliable, and calculated correctly across all reports.
  • Assist and mentor the support team with SQL query composition and data literacy.
  • Conduct A/B tests to measure the effect of Support and Trust & Safety initiatives on relevant departmental and broader business metrics.
  • Analyze the correlation between support quality, bug-related friction, and subsequent player behavior, with a strong focus on VIP cohorts.
  • Partner with the safety team to analyze data for fraud detection, including social engineering and account sharing.
  • Perform time-series forecasting for workload volume based on the LiveOps calendar to optimize shift headcount and SLA coverage.
  • As a future ambition, build and maintain an early warning predictive model for VIP player churn.


What you have

  • Strong SQL skills and data modeling experience
  • Experience with BI tools (e.g. Tableau or equivalents)
  • Proven experience in product or operational analytics
  • Ability to design analytical events and metrics aligned with product goals
  • Strong analytical thinking and ability to translate data into actionable insights
  • Confident knowledge of mathematical statistics and probability theory
  • Hands-on experience with causal inference and A/B testing
  • Patience and willingness to advocate for data literacy and help operational teams learn basic data concepts


Nice to haves

  • Hands-on experience with ClickHouse and dbt
  • Experience analyzing LiveOps features, complex game economies, or VIP player behavior, ideally in mid-core or 4x strategy games.
  • Experience working with Customer Support or Trust & Safety (fraud detection, toxicity)
  • Experience working with large player bases and complex event-driven systems


Where you’ll work

We value face-to-face communication, and we strongly believe that genuine human interactions move us closer to our ambitious goal. This is part of our culture, and this is why this job offers no remote or hybrid work opportunities.

You will be working in Limassol, Cyprus. If you’re not from around here, fear not! Our dedicated relocation team will provide comprehensive support helping you and your family move to Limassol.


What we offer

  • The best team in the world!
  • Salary of 5000-7000 eur net/month + 13th salary.
  • Fully-covered relocation to Limassol, Cyprus for you and your family.
  • A modern and sunny office space close to the sea.
  • Private health insurance for you and your family members.
  • A monthly school allowance for your children at pre-school and school-age.
  • Lunch compensation.
  • Personal development opportunities paid for by the company: professional and language courses, books, etc.
  • Bonuses for outstanding results, years worked, getting married, and the birth of a child.
  • Monthly office parties and epic, annual offsite company birthday and Christmas parties.

Title: Segment Developer Data Centre (f/m/d)
Description:
E.ON Energy Infrastructure Solutions GmbH

E.ON Energy Infrastructure Solutions GmbH (EIS), a subsidiary of the E.ON Group, is a multinational integrator of managed energy solutions for B2B customers and the public sector. We make energy more efficient, sustainable, and cost-effective across Europe with tailor-made energy generation and management systems. Our focus: energy efficiency, decentralized solutions, flexibility, virtual power plants, and battery storage to optimize energy use and minimize CO₂ emissions.

At E.ON diversity matters. We welcome all people and are convinced that differences make us stronger. Become part of our inclusive and diverse company culture! To create equal opportunities for everyone we offer our positions in full- or part-time.

Ready to become a Playmaker of the energy transition? Join our team in Berlin, Essen, München and apply online as Segment Developer Data Centre (f/m/d). Are you brave enough to drive progress and forge new paths? Let’s make it work and create real impact together. We can’t wait to meet you! It’s on us, to make new energy work.

Your Responsibilities

Growth and Strategy Account Management:


Strategically develop and manage international key accounts in the data center segment (hyperscalers, colocation, neocloud providers).
Build and maintain long-term customer relationships at C-suite level.
Apply structured sales methodologies such as MEDDIC, Miller Heimann, Relationship Selling, and Value-Based Selling to drive systematic business development
Having an extensive and growing network within the Data Center market as well as a broad professional background with strong sales and commercial experience and knowledge of the energy industry and large energy infrastructure solutions.


Segment Strategy & Market Development


Co-develop and implement the central segment strategy for data centers in close collaboration with the Segment Lead.
Develop and execute go-to-market strategies for target markets, in conjunction with the Marketing function, and build use cases
Develop and establish Customer Value Propositions together with our Solutions Factory and responsible business units
Actively participate in relevant associations (e.g., DCA UK, GDA, EUDCA, Bitkom) and coordinate lobbying on national and EU levels


International Key Account Management Community & Knowledge Sharing


Build and maintain an internal cross-country data center community to foster knowledge exchange and synchronize activities across business units.
Collaborate closely with functional units and all relevant central functions


Your Qualifications


University degree, preferably in Economics, Engineering or similar
Several years of experience in international key account management or business development, ideally in energy infrastructure and with strong connection with data centers
Strong network within data center segment
Proven track record in project management
Proactive hands-on mentality
Excellent stakeholder management and proactive management of decision-making processes
Experience in organizing and coordinating cross country teams or similar growth platforms
Strong understanding of the economic, technical and legal context of complex energy infrastructure solutions
Strong entrepreneurial and sales mindset


Benefits


Flexibility: hybrid working model, flexible working hours, sabbatical or additional holiday options that enable a good work-life balance
Working abroad: up to 20 days in the European Economic Area
Flat hierarchies: interdisciplinary and highly collaborative working style that offers room for your own ideas
Modern working environment: workplace in line with digital and ergonomic standards
Personal development: lifelong independent learning utilising a wide range of opportunities to work with the latest technology and state-of-the-art training
Family service: services in the areas of childcare, holiday care, care support or everyday assistance
Nutrition & health: wide selection of fresh food and drinks in our subsidised bistro and canteen as well as various health services (e.g. physiotherapy, flu vaccinations, mental health)
Corporate benefits: employee share programme, pension scheme, employee discounts, special insurance (and much more)
Central location: excellent public transport connections, free parking spaces and charging stations for electric vehicles
Individual mobility: from private car and bicycle leasing to subsidised job tickets


Inklusion

Uns ist wichtig, Menschen mit Behinderung ein faires Bewerbungsverfahren zu ermöglichen, in dem sie ihr Bestes zeigen können. Daher wird unsere Schwerbehindertenvertretung frühzeitig in das Verfahren einbezogen. Bitte teile uns über das Bewerbungsformular mit, ob du technische oder organisatorische Anpassungen für das Bewerbungsverfahren benötigst. Wir stellen diese gern zur Verfügung.

Wenn es dir nicht möglich ist, dich online zu bewerben und du stattdessen mit jemandem sprechen möchtest, kontaktiere bitte deinen lokalen Recruiter.

Das zeichnet E.ON als Arbeitgeber aus


Sinnhaftigkeit Gemeinsam ebnen wir den Weg für die grüne Energiewende in Europa und ermöglichen der Gesellschaft und unseren Kunden CO2-neutral zu werden.
Nachhaltigkeit Wir machen Energie sauberer und intelligenter und ermöglichen unseren Kunden Zugang zu einer nachhaltigen Strom- und Wärmeversorgung.
Teamgeist Wir fördern eine integrative Kultur, in der sich jeder wertgeschätzt fühlt, sein volles Potenzial entfalten und zum Erfolg von E.ON beitragen kann.
Wertschätzung Wir schätzen den Beitrag jedes Einzelnen. Wir erkennen individuelle Leistungen an und belohnen die Erfolge, die wir als Team erzielen.
Work Life Balance Familie oder Karriere? Bei uns keine Frage. Ob Gleitzeit, Teilzeit oder hybrides Arbeiten: Bei uns findest du für jede Lebenssituation das passende Arbeitsmodell.
Gesundheit Bei uns profitierst du von vielfältigen Angeboten rund um Gesundheit, Vorsorge und Wohlbefinden.
Netzwerk Dein Einstieg bei E.ON öffnet dir die Tür zu unzähligen Möglichkeiten – regional und international.
Entwicklung Ob Fach- oder Führungslaufbahn: Mit unseren individuellen Weiterbildungsangeboten entwickelst du dich fachlich und persönlich.
Onboarding Wir unterstützen dich dabei, E.ON kennenzulernen und dich schnell in deinem neuen Arbeitsumfeld zurechtzufinden. So fällt dir der Start bei uns leicht.

Title: Working Student - Data Science & Robotics Machine Learning
Description:
Company Description

RoboService solves the "Last Mile" of humanoid robotics. While manufacturers provide the hardware and foundation models, these general-purpose "brains" are not yet ready for the specific, accurate tasks of a production line. We bridge that gap.




We don't build foundation models from scratch; we operationalize them. We take state-of-the-art VLA models and fine-tune them for reliability, speed, and precision in real-world manufacturing environments.




We are not a research lab; we are shipping code that moves atoms. Launched in July 2025 by repeat founders with successful exits, we are looking for engineers who want to take robots out of the lab and onto the factory floor.




Role Description

We are looking for an intern / working student who joins our Robotics Machine Learning Engineering team. You will bridge the gap between "remote controlled hardware" and "autonomous robots". We work with robotics manufacturers that provide great hardware and a model infrastructure, but are missing the software to fine-tune the models on a specific task. Your primary focus will be establishing training pipelines - from teleoperation data collection to fine-tuning VLA models and utilizing Reinforcement Learning (RL) to fine-tune complex manipulation tasks.




You won't be writing papers. You will be:




Building data pipelines to capture and analyze training data
Fine-tuning VLA models (open source and proprietary models from our partners)
Managing the Data Pipeline and overseeing the Teleoperation activities. Manage large datasets required for fine-tuning models.
Reinforcement Learning (RL): Apply RL techniques to fine-tune robot actions, ensuring robust performance when the robot works on the production line.
Utilize high-fidelity simulation environments (MuJoCo, NVIDIA Isaac Sim, Omniverse) to train and test agents before deploying them to physical hardware. (Sim2Real)




Qualifications

Education: Currently pursuing your Master’s degree or PhD in Robotics, Computer Science, Data Science, Artificial Intelligence, or a related field.
Experience: Machine Learning, Deep learning, or embodied AI. Hands-On Robotics Experience.
Core ML Skills: Strong proficiency in Python and deep learning frameworks (PyTorch, TensorFlow, or JAX). Experience with Transformers and VLA architectures is highly desirable.
Simulation: Proven experience with physics-based simulators, specifically NVIDIA Isaac Sim or similar (Gazebo, MuJoCo).
Data Engineering: Ability to analyze and manage large datasets, automate data cleaning, and build training pipelines.
Proficiency in English




What We Offer




Path to Full-Time: After a successful internship, we aim to extend a full-time offer.
Berlin based: We’re an in-person team and we work out of our office in downtown Berlin. If you're not in Berlin yet, be prepared to move here before the start of the job.
Access to Cutting Edge Tech: Work daily on the latest software and hardware that will shape our future.
Impact: Work in real projects with manufacturers to bring robots onto manufacturing floors.
Startup Environment: We are a fast-moving startup with high growth ambitions.
Team of Experts: We are a team of experts where every voice matters. Work with professionals that are leading their field.




Apply now!

Title: Senior Analyst, In-Field Repair Quality
Description:
As a global leader in micromobility, Lime is on a mission to build a future where transportation is shared, affordable and carbon-free. A Time Magazine 100 Most Influential Company, Lime has powered more than one billion rides in close to 30 countries across five continents, spurring a new generation of clean alternatives to car ownership. Learn more at li.me.

Lime is hiring a Senior Analyst to join our Global Operations team. In this role, you'll be at the forefront of improving in-field repair quality performance— developing key metrics, building tools that drive real-time visibility, and delivering targeted analysis that informs operational decisions and strategic opportunities.

You'll serve as the functional bridge between Local Operations and our Product organization, translating operational needs into structured data requirements and tooling improvements. You'll collaborate closely with Local Operations, Product, Data Science, and Hardware teams to help shape the future of Lime's in-field repair quality across all of our operations as we continue to scale.

The ideal candidate is highly analytical, technically strong, and passionate about using data to solve complex problems. You're equally comfortable partnering with local operations teams and product or engineering stakeholders — and you know how to both structure a process and build a dashboard. If you have 3+ years of experience in business analytics or operations, strong data analysis and visualization skills, and a drive to influence outcomes through insight, we'd love to hear from you.

This is a hybrid role open to candidates based anywhere in Europe (London, Berlin, Paris, Munich, or Copenhagen preferred), within 120 minutes of a Lime warehouse. The role requires occasional travel (~10%) to Lime warehouses or other locations for team meetings.

What You’ll Do


Improve visibility into in-field repair quality: Define and maintain metrics to track repair performance, effectiveness, compliance, efficiency, and ROI — and build tooling to surface these insights in real time across operations
Build models and dashboards: Use SQL, Tableau, and spreadsheets to develop repair volume forecasting models, quality compliance dashboards, and performance reports for local operations teams — with robust logic, clearly defined assumptions, and intuitive interfaces
Monitor trends and diagnose performance gaps: Track repair efficiency and quality compliance over time, identify root causes behind variances or emerging trends, and recommend actionable solutions at both tactical and strategic levels
Turn operational complexity into structured clarity: Bridge Local Operations and Product/Tech by converting messy, real-world business needs into crisp specs, user stories, data requirements, and SOPs that teams can actually execute against
Formalize and improve operational processes: Map and document quality workflows, surface inefficiencies, and partner with Local Operations to define and roll out improved standard operating procedures
Collaborate with partner teams to improve efficiency: Provide insight and targeted analysis to troubleshoot issues or highlight opportunities. Partner with Operations leadership and local teams to land recommendations and drive action through to resolution
Drive continuous improvement in data analytics capabilities across global operations: Contribute to a culture of data-driven decision making and analytical excellence through peer collaboration, knowledge sharing, and support


About You


Extremely strong proficiency in data analytics, including advanced SQL, spreadsheet modeling, and data visualization (e.g. Tableau or equivalent).
Bachelor's degree in Business, Mathematics, Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field, or equivalent industry experience
3+ years of experience in business analytics or operations analytics, with demonstrated ability to build reports and dashboards used by operational stakeholders
Experience translating business needs into functional data and reporting requirements, ideally working closely with a Product or Engineering team
Ability to structure and document operational processes, comfortable with ambiguity and working from first principles when standard frameworks don't exist
Curious mindset to spot trends in data, deep-dive root causes, and generate proposals for both tactical and strategic solutions
Clear verbal and written communicator, with strong collaboration and influencing skills across both field teams and technical stakeholders


Preferred Experience


Python, Google Apps Script, or other automation/scripting capabilities are a strong plus
Previous experience in a fast-paced, scaling operations environment (gig economy, last-mile, field services, or similar)
Experience working closely with Product or Engineering teams on tooling/product improvements


If you want to make an impact, Lime is the place for you. Not sure if you meet all the qualifications? If this role excites you we encourage you to apply. Explore all opportunities on our career page.

Lime is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We believe different perspectives help us grow and achieve more. That’s why we’re dedicated to building and developing a team that reflects a wider range of backgrounds, abilities, identities, and experiences. If you require a reasonable accommodation during the application or hiring process, please email recruiting-operations@li.me for assistance.

Title: Data Engineer (m/f/d) – Berlin
Description:
Your Mission

Your Impact

At Bees & Bears, data is the backbone of everything we do. As our first dedicated Data Engineer, you'll build the foundation that allows us to scale - cleanly, reliably, and without adding operational chaos.

Right now, our data lives in silos across tools, teams, and processes. Manual work fills the gaps, and that's a bottleneck we need to solve. You'll design and own the infrastructure that changes that: a single source of truth that powers decisions across Risk, Finance, Product, and Operations.

You'll work closely with our Engineering, Product, Risk, and Finance teams, as well as directly with our leadership, to understand what data they need and make sure they can actually use it.

Your Mission


Build and maintain robust data pipelines across all core systems
Design and implement our company data warehouse from the ground up
Integrate data from internal systems, APIs, and third-party tools
Ensure data reliability, consistency, and accessibility across the company
Enable teams with clean, structured data for faster and better decision-making
Define data ownership, naming conventions, and quality standards that scale


Your Talent


Must-have:
Strong SQL and Python skills - you're genuinely comfortable writing complex queries and automating data workflows
Hands-on experience building and maintaining data pipelines end to end
Experience with data warehouse setup and data modeling
Proven ability to work independently and build systems from scratch
Strong preference:
Experience with modern data stack tools such as dbt, Airflow, Snowflake, or BigQuery
Background in a startup or high-growth environment
Experience collaborating with Product, Risk, or Finance teams
Nice to have:
Experience with BI tools and dashboarding
Basic understanding of financial data or fintech processes
Experience with data quality, testing, and monitoring frameworks
Familiarity with data privacy and security requirements

Our Offer

Growth & Culture

We move fast, we're building a lot from scratch, and we're learning something new every day. You'll be part of a team that doesn't just work, it truly builds something, with a strong sense of ownership and genuine team spirit.

Impact

Your job has a direct impact on the energy transition. Period.

And because this is important to us, we offer extra days off for climate activism.

Flexibility

We work in a hybrid, trust-based environment.

Working from home is the norm for us, two days a week are standard and flexible. We structure the rest in whatever way best supports collaboration and focus within the team.

And if it works for you and your team, you can also work from other locations, workations are possible here.

Perks

You'll receive a flexible bonus that you can use however you like, sports, culture, travel, whatever works for you.

Food & Energy

Long days happen. When the office is running, so is the food: just order something to the office via Wolt.

Team & Vibe

We take our mission seriously, but we don't take ourselves too seriously.

Offsites, team nights, and our "Bees & Beers" are just as much a part of the job as the work itself.

About Us

At Bees & Bears, we’re a Climate FinTech on a mission to unlock capital for the energy transition. We believe finance is the key to accelerating the shift toward a sustainable future. The challenge ahead is massive: by 2050, the world needs to invest €150 trillion (!) to reach climate neutrality. 65% of this investment will need to be financed through loans.

Our mission is to channel capital into the sustainable economy — to accelerate the transition and make the world greener.

What we do:

We work closely with installers of climate hardware such as solar systems, energy storage, and heat pumps — enabling their customers to finance installations easily through monthly installments. Our platform provides the technical and financial foundation to make this happen. In doing so, we’re actively accelerating the energy transition — for households, for our partners, and for the planet.

We’ve recently secured €500 million in funding commitments to green 25,000 households over the next two years. Prior to that, we raised two funding rounds backed by several renowned VCs — and we’ve been growing rapidly ever since.

Sound like the perfect job for you?

We look forward to your application and to getting to know you!

At Bees & Bears, we place a high value on equal opportunity and are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive work environment. We strive to create a friendly, safe, and welcoming environment for all our employees, regardless of ethnic origin, gender, gender identity and expression, sexual orientation, disability and abilities of any kind, physical appearance, social background, marital status, age, or religion (or lack thereof).

Even if you don’t meet the requirements 100%, we still encourage you to apply.

You don’t want an ordinary job. You want to build something that truly matters. Be part of shaping it from day one, move fast and feel your impact. In a startup that doesn’t ask if something is possible – but how fast. Then you’ve come to the right place!

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
As a Data Engineer at PERGOLUX, you will build and own the data pipelines that enable fast, reliable operational insights across the business. Your work ensures the company can trust its data, automate confidently, and make high-impact decisions in real time. You will join a lean, senior data team where reliability, clarity, and business value—not technical complexity—define success.

At PERGOLUX, we combine Scandinavian-inspired design with a collaborative, ambitious culture grounded in integrity, innovation, and unity.

What You’ll Do


Build and maintain a dependable ingestion and delivery layer that moves data into Snowflake and feeds operational tools with high accuracy and low maintenance overhead.
Own the ingestion backbone across systems such as HubSpot, Klaviyo, Google Ads, and Canary7, selecting the tools and approaches that maximize stability and simplicity.
Design monitoring, observability, and alerting that eliminate silent failures and give stakeholders full visibility into data health and freshness.
Define pragmatic orchestration patterns that ensure scalable, cost-efficient, and resilient pipelines—introducing new tooling only when it clearly reduces failure modes or maintenance costs.
Lead simplification initiatives within the first 90 days by improving stability, reducing pipeline breaks, and implementing a transparent, well-instrumented data platform.


What You’ll Bring


A pragmatic mindset focused on simplicity, reliability, and business value over unnecessary technical complexity.
Strong systems thinking and the ability to evaluate downstream impact when designing pipelines and data flows.
Fluency in Python, SQL, and Git, with a deep understanding of production-grade data engineering concepts such as data contracts, schema evolution, idempotency, and failure-state management.
Ability to create force-multiplying infrastructure that removes ambiguity, eliminates manual work, and accelerates Analytics Engineering workflows.
A disciplined approach to protecting platform stability, reducing technical debt, and ensuring predictable, trustworthy data delivery.


Benefits


Competitive Salary & Bonus Structure – Your success is rewarded with strong compensation and performance-based bonuses.
Real Ownership – Take meaningful responsibility in your role and directly influence key business outcomes.
International Team – Collaborate with passionate colleagues across Europe, gaining exposure to diverse perspectives and ideas.
Outdoor Lifestyle Discounts – Enjoy exclusive discounts on PERGOLUX products.
Fast-Track Career Opportunities – As we scale quickly, you’ll have significant opportunities to grow into senior leadership roles.
Team Culture – Be part of regular team events, retreats, and a culture that celebrates wins—big and small.
Bike Leasing Program – Benefit from a sustainable and cost-effective bike leasing option to support commuting and an active lifestyle.


How to Apply

Ready to shape the data foundation of a fast-scaling global brand? Apply through our career channels:

PERGOLUX LinkedIn

PERGOLUX Career Page
