Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
The Company

Cover Genius is a Series E insurtech that protects the global customers of the world’s largest digital companies including Booking Holdings, owner of Priceline, Kayak and Booking.com, Intuit, Uber, Hopper, Ryanair, Turkish Airlines, Descartes ShipRush, Zip and SeatGeek. We’re also available at Amazon, Flipkart, eBay, Wayfair and SE Asia’s largest company, Shopee. Our partners integrate with XCover, our award-winning insurance distribution platform, to embed protection for millions of customers worldwide each year.

Our team and products have been recognized with dozens of awards including by the Financial Times which ranked Cover Genius as the #1 fastest-growing company in APAC in 2020. Our diverse team across 20+ countries and many language groups commit itself to diverse cultural programs, in particular “CG Gives” which makes social entrepreneurs out of us all and funds development initiatives in global communities.

Our People are

Bold, Authentic, Purposeful and Inspired

Our People are not

Perfect, Traditional, Complacent or Cautious

About The Role

We are seeking a highly analytical, adaptable, and impact-driven Senior Data Analyst to join our growing Product Operations team. This pivotal role will embed within our product domains and problem spaces, acting as a critical partner to Product Managers in driving growth and optimizing the end-to-end customer experience.

You will be obsessed with leveraging data to support teams with strategic product decisions, identify growth opportunities, and ensure operational excellence at scale. This role is ideal for someone excited by continuous change, eager to tackle diverse challenges across various product initiatives, and committed to a data-driven culture

Key Responsibilities

What will your day look like?


Empower Product Teams to be Data Driven: As a strategic partner, you will provide cross-product insights and connect the dots across multiple programs. As the "truth seeker," you will objectively analyze data and identify opportunities. You will design, develop, and own insightful dashboards and reports that provide visibility into key product metrics, user engagement patterns, and feature performance. These tools will empower product teams to be data-led and make informed decisions by ensuring Product Managers have accurate information.
Support Data-Driven Decision Making: You will translate ambiguous business questions into well-defined data problems and deliver actionable recommendations through clean, compelling data narratives. You will collaborate closely with Product Managers to understand user needs and ensure that all decisions are informed by robust data and analysis
Drive Product Growth with Actionable Insights: You will perform deep-dive analysis to unpack insights and recommend solutions to top-of-mind problems the business is facing. Your analysis will go beyond simple reporting of "what happened" to provide a clear understanding of "why it happened", supporting product teams in leveraging data for strategic initiatives and decision-making, influencing product strategy and feature prioritization.
Champion Continuous Improvement & Automation: Actively participate in retrospectives and contribute to a culture of learning and iteration, using data to identify areas for process and product improvement. Drive automation and productisation of analytical solutions, transforming client questions into scalable analytical solutions. Proactively identify data quality issues or opportunities for improvement in analytics tooling and reporting.
Collaborate Cross-Functionally: Work closely with diverse teams, including product, marketing, data engineering, and other relevant teams, to align efforts toward growth initiatives and ensure shared understanding of product performance.
Stakeholder Communication: Communicate findings to stakeholders at various levels, adjusting content and complexity to suit the audience, and translating complex analyses into clear, actionable takeaways tailored for both technical and non-technical audiences.
Ensure Accountability: Ensure accountability for analytics quality and successful delivery of key projects with measurable client outcomes. Support quarterly and annual planning efforts with data inputs, forecasting trends, and key performance analytics.


Experience & Skills

What you will bring:


4+ years of experience as a Data Analyst, preferably within a product-led organization or supporting product teams.
Strong SQL proficiency is essential for data extraction, manipulation, and analysis.
Experience with data modeling and transformation tools like dbt (Data Build Tool).
Familiarity with workflow orchestration tools such as Airflow.
Proficiency in building dashboards and reports using business intelligence tools (e.g., Superset) to visualize data and communicate insights effectively.
Demonstrated ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights for non-technical stakeholders.
A strong understanding of product metrics, user behavior, and sales funnels.
A proactive and curious mindset, with a relentless focus on identifying opportunities for improvement and driving measurable outcomes.
Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to influence decisions through data.
Adaptability and excitement for working in a fast-paced, evolving environment, embracing new challenges across different product areas.
Basic understanding of Python for data analysis and scripting is a plus.


To Help Us Level Up, You Will Have/be


Data-Driven Mindset: Demands data to inform every significant operational decision.
Problem Solver: Approaches challenges with a solution-oriented attitude.
Accountable & Results-Oriented: Takes full ownership of work and focuses on tangible execution and measurable improvements.
Collaborative Communicator: Fosters clear, timely communication and values constructive feedback.

The Company

Cover Genius is a Series E insurtech that protects the global customers of the world’s largest digital companies including Booking Holdings, owner of Priceline, Kayak and Booking.com, Intuit, Uber, Hopper, Ryanair, Turkish Airlines, Descartes ShipRush, Zip and SeatGeek. We’re also available at Amazon, Flipkart, eBay, Wayfair and SE Asia’s largest company, Shopee. Our partners integrate with XCover, our award-winning insurance distribution platform, to embed protection for millions of customers worldwide each year.

Our team and products have been recognized with dozens of awards including by the Financial Times which ranked Cover Genius as the #1 fastest-growing company in APAC in 2020. Our diverse team across 20+ countries and many language groups commit itself to diverse cultural programs, in particular “CG Gives” which makes social entrepreneurs out of us all and funds development initiatives in global communities.

Our People are

Bold, Authentic, Purposeful and Inspired

Our People are not

Perfect, Traditional, Complacent or Cautious

About The Role

We are seeking a highly analytical, adaptable, and impact-driven Senior Data Analyst to join our growing Product Operations team. This pivotal role will embed within our product domains and problem spaces, acting as a critical partner to Product Managers in driving growth and optimizing the end-to-end customer experience.

You will be obsessed with leveraging data to support teams with strategic product decisions, identify growth opportunities, and ensure operational excellence at scale. This role is ideal for someone excited by continuous change, eager to tackle diverse challenges across various product initiatives, and committed to a data-driven culture

Key Responsibilities

What will your day look like?

  • Empower Product Teams to be Data Driven: As a strategic partner, you will provide cross-product insights and connect the dots across multiple programs. As the "truth seeker," you will objectively analyze data and identify opportunities. You will design, develop, and own insightful dashboards and reports that provide visibility into key product metrics, user engagement patterns, and feature performance. These tools will empower product teams to be data-led and make informed decisions by ensuring Product Managers have accurate information.
  • Support Data-Driven Decision Making: You will translate ambiguous business questions into well-defined data problems and deliver actionable recommendations through clean, compelling data narratives. You will collaborate closely with Product Managers to understand user needs and ensure that all decisions are informed by robust data and analysis
  • Drive Product Growth with Actionable Insights: You will perform deep-dive analysis to unpack insights and recommend solutions to top-of-mind problems the business is facing. Your analysis will go beyond simple reporting of "what happened" to provide a clear understanding of "why it happened", supporting product teams in leveraging data for strategic initiatives and decision-making, influencing product strategy and feature prioritization.
  • Champion Continuous Improvement & Automation: Actively participate in retrospectives and contribute to a culture of learning and iteration, using data to identify areas for process and product improvement. Drive automation and productisation of analytical solutions, transforming client questions into scalable analytical solutions. Proactively identify data quality issues or opportunities for improvement in analytics tooling and reporting.
  • Collaborate Cross-Functionally: Work closely with diverse teams, including product, marketing, data engineering, and other relevant teams, to align efforts toward growth initiatives and ensure shared understanding of product performance.
  • Stakeholder Communication: Communicate findings to stakeholders at various levels, adjusting content and complexity to suit the audience, and translating complex analyses into clear, actionable takeaways tailored for both technical and non-technical audiences.
  • Ensure Accountability: Ensure accountability for analytics quality and successful delivery of key projects with measurable client outcomes. Support quarterly and annual planning efforts with data inputs, forecasting trends, and key performance analytics.

Experience & Skills

What you will bring:

  • 4+ years of experience as a Data Analyst, preferably within a product-led organization or supporting product teams.
  • Strong SQL proficiency is essential for data extraction, manipulation, and analysis.
  • Experience with data modeling and transformation tools like dbt (Data Build Tool).
  • Familiarity with workflow orchestration tools such as Airflow.
  • Proficiency in building dashboards and reports using business intelligence tools (e.g., Superset) to visualize data and communicate insights effectively.
  • Demonstrated ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights for non-technical stakeholders.
  • A strong understanding of product metrics, user behavior, and sales funnels.
  • A proactive and curious mindset, with a relentless focus on identifying opportunities for improvement and driving measurable outcomes.
  • Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to influence decisions through data.
  • Adaptability and excitement for working in a fast-paced, evolving environment, embracing new challenges across different product areas.
  • Basic understanding of Python for data analysis and scripting is a plus.

  • Data-Driven Mindset: Demands data to inform every significant operational decision.

Title: SOC Analyst – Level 1
Description:
Position Overview

We are seeking a SOC Analyst – Level 1 specializing in XDR Monitoring. You will be responsible for 24/7/365 monitoring of client environments, triaging security alerts, supporting incident response, and contributing to continuous improvement of threat detection and response capabilities. You will work closely with SOC engineers, technical account managers, and client stakeholders to ensure rapid and effective handling of security incidents.

As a SOC Analyst, you will conduct log analysis, validate alerts, investigate suspicious activity, and escalate confirmed incidents in alignment with established playbooks. You will also perform and proactive monitoring to identify attacks that may bypass automated detections. You will perform analysis of security events generated by the XDR and tools. A key part of your responsibilities will be to document investigations, provide detailed reporting, and deliver clear communication to internal teams and clients. This is a rotation-based position with shift work, requiring reliability, collaboration, and a strong commitment to ensuring continuous security coverage.

Responsibilities


Monitor and analyze security events and alerts using XDR and related platforms, ensuring continuous 24/7/365 surveillance of client environments for potential threats and incidents.
Perform alarm triage, Assess and investigate alerts for signs of attacks, compromises, or suspicious activity, escalating incidents according to defined SLA priorities (P1–P5)
Analyze threats, investigate anomalous behavior, and advise on basic incident response steps.
Recommend and contribute to the fine-tuning of detection rules, correlation logic, and playbooks to improve accuracy.
Correlate alerts with threat intelligence feeds and vulnerability data to assess real risk.
Conduct proactive hunts across endpoints, network, and cloud environments to identify threats not detected by automated tools.
Collaborate with technical account managers and participate in technical calls to report on threat and SLA compliance trends.
Maintain dashboard updates and ensure continuous improvement of monitoring processes.
Participate in containment procedures and support blocking/isolation of indicators of compromise (IoCs).
Willingness and ability to participate in shift-based rotations (including nights, weekends, and holidays) as part of a 24/7 SOC team.


Qualifications


Already have a valid Federal Government security clearance or eligible to have one.
1-2+ years of experience in a SOC or cybersecurity analyst role (XDR/SIEM/EDR experience preferred).
Strong knowledge of XDR platforms, security event monitoring, and incident response workflows.
Understanding of threat detection technologies, alarm triage, and reporting best practices.
Experience with ticketing systems (e.g., Jira) and workflow automation.
Strong analytical skills and attention to detail.
Ability to work in a 24/7/365 operational environment, including shift work.
Knowledge of compliance reporting and security best practices.
Industry-recognized cybersecurity certifications such as CCFA, CCFR, CCFH, SC-200, SC-900, AZ-500, GCIH, CEH, or similar are a plus.
Solid understanding of SOC operations, incident response, and threat detection methodologies.
Knowledge of MITRE ATT&CK framework and applying it to Investigation, Monitoring & Response.
Ability to work independently, take initiative, and lead efforts with minimal supervision.
Excellent English communication skills (written and verbal) to interact effectively with Canadian clients and internal teams.
A degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Cybersecurity, or equivalent experience.
Strong knowledge of adversary tactics, leveraging MITRE ATT&CK for advanced threat detection.
Proficiency in security monitoring tools, log analysis, and forensic investigations.
Ability to work in a high-pressure environment, making quick and informed decisions during security incidents.


Compensation


$60-70K


“Final compensation will be based on the successful candidate’s skills, experience, qualifications, and contract conditions.”

Vacancy


We have 3 (three) available positions


At Calian, we do not use Artificial Intelligence (AI) to screen or evaluate candidates. AI tools may support backend administrative tasks, but they do not influence hiring decisions. All evaluations and decisions are made by real people on our recruitment team and hiring managers. All applicants are reviewed and only those chosen for an interview will be contacted by our recruiting team.

Title: Business Reporting Analyst
Description:
Job Information

Job Title: Business Reporting Analyst

Job Requisition ID: 85248

Ministry: Alberta Justice

Location: Edmonton

Full or Part-Time: Full-Time

Hours of Work: 36.25 hours/week

Permanent/Temporary: Permanent

Scope: Open

Closing Date: July 3, 2026

Classification: Program Services 4

Salary: $3,148.20 to $4,126.82 bi-weekly ($82,168 - $107,710 per year)

The Ministry of Justice works to ensure a fair and accessible justice system for all Albertans where the rule of law is upheld, and government undertakings are administered according to law. In collaboration with the judiciary, law enforcement, public safety, and health, the ministry provides programs and services that ensure access to justice and support the needs of Albertans involved in the justice system. Follow this link to find out more about our department https://www.alberta.ca/justice.aspx .

The Strategy, Support and Integrated Initiatives (SSII) Division provides strategic and integrated support for Justice services and ministry priorities. Within SSII, the Modernization, IMT and Research (MIR) Branch delivers data, reporting, research, evaluation, and performance measurement services to support evidence-informed decision-making, accountability, and continuous improvement across the justice system.

The Reporting, Research and Evaluation (RRE) unit provides operational and strategic reporting, business intelligence, research, evaluation, performance measurement, statistical analysis, and information products that support program management, policy development, and service improvement. The unit also contributes to the development and enhancement of data and reporting systems to improve access to reliable and actionable information.

Role Responsibilities

Reporting to the Manager, Business & Strategic Reporting within the Modernization, IMT & Research branch, the Business Reporting Analyst is responsible for designing, developing, implementing, and supporting data products and solutions to meet departmental decision-making needs.

You will design and develop reporting and analytical products using data visualization and statistical analysis techniques to support operational and strategic decision-making. You will also contribute to the development and enhancement of business intelligence systems using your knowledge of data warehousing and business analysis concepts.

This role includes a research and analytical component, requiring the ability to extract relevant information through effective engagement with business areas, strong knowledge of legacy systems, and a high degree of attention to detail. A spirit of curiosity and innovation is valued, with opportunities to explore new methods and tools to solve complex issues.

Core Accountabilities Of The Positions Include


Designing, developing and maintaining dashboards, reports, and other business intelligence products to support operational, strategic, and policy decision-making.
Engaging with stakeholders to identify reporting, analytics, and performance measurement requirements, translating business needs into effective data products and solutions.
Collecting, preparing, validating, and analyzing data from multiple sources to support reporting, performance measurement, research, and evaluation activities.
Applying statistical, analytical, and data visualization techniques to identify trends, generate insights, and address complex business questions.
Querying data, aggregating results, and developing reports to respond to ad hoc requests involving sensitive and high-impact information.
Supporting the development and maintenance of data governance practices, business rules, reporting standards, and documentation to ensure data quality, consistency, and reliability.


Contributing to the enhancement of business intelligence systems, reporting processes, and analytical capabilities through continuous improvement, innovation, and user support.

If this sounds like a position you would thrive in, we encourage you to apply!

Please click on this link to view the job description for this position.

APS Competencies

Competencies are behaviors that are essential to reach our goals in serving Albertans. We encourage you to have an in depth understanding of the competencies that are required for this opportunity and to be prepared to demonstrate them during the recruitment process.

This Link Will Assist You With Understanding Competencies

https://www.alberta.ca/system/files/custom_downloaded_images/psc-alberta-public-service-competency-model.pdf .

The following competencies will support your success in this role:


Systems Thinking: Applies a broad, strategic perspective to analyze data and program outcomes, considering interrelationships, emerging trends, and long-term impacts. Evaluates the implications of different options and identifies potential risks or unintended consequences to support informed decision-making and continuous improvement. Accurately analyze, identify and define Ministry initiatives and priorities to ensure alignment with larger government strategies.
Agility: Demonstrates adaptability in a dynamic environment by proactively identifying opportunities to improve processes, adjusting to evolving priorities, and maintaining a calm, solution-focused approach while working creatively within established guidelines.
Creative Problem Solving: Using analytical thinking and curiosity to understand complex data and reporting challenges, asking thoughtful questions, exploring new approaches, and leveraging diverse data sources to identify insights and improve reporting processes and program outcomes.
Drive for Results: Delivers high-quality outcomes by leading effective discussions with system partners, synthesizing complex information from diverse sources, and applying strong analytical and statistical expertise to develop practical, evidence-based solutions.


Qualifications

Requirements:


University degree in a related field (e.g., Social Sciences, Mathematics, Statistics)
Minimum 4 years of progressively responsible related experience in data analysis and reporting.


Assets


Experience using business intelligence, data visualization, querying, and analytical tools (e.g., Power BI, SQL, R, Python, SSRS, Excel, VBA).
Experience developing reports, dashboards, performance measures, or analytical products to support operational and strategic decision-making.
Experience applying statistical and analytical techniques to identify trends, generate insights, and support evidence-informed decision making.
Experience communicating complex, technical, or sensitive information to diverse audiences and building collaborative relationships with stakeholders.
Experience working with justice system data and/or complex, multi-source datasets.


Equivalencies will be considered based on one-year experience for one year of education or one year of education for one year of experience.

Examples


A related 2-year related diploma and 6 years of related experience.
A related 2-year master's and 2 years of related experience.
A related 1-year certificate and 7 years related experience.
No post secondary education and a minimum of 8 years related experience.


Please outline how your qualifications relate to the role in your cover letter. Providing a cover letter is strongly encouraged as your written skills will be evaluated through your cover letter.

Minimum recruitment standards outline the minimum education and experience required for appointment to a job classification. Refer to https://www.alberta.ca/alberta-public-service-minimum-recruitment-standards .

Notes


Full time permanent position located in Edmonton at Oxbridge Place.
Hours of work: 7.25 hours per day / 36.25 hours per week (Monday – Friday).
The successful candidate will be required to undergo a security screening.


Any costs associated with obtaining the required documents/checks as noted or interview travel expenses, will be the responsibility of the candidate. Out-of-province applicants can obtain the required documents/checks from the province they currently reside in.

Links and information on what the GoA have to offer to prospective employees.


Working for the Alberta Public Service - https://www.alberta.ca/advantages-working-for-alberta-public-service.aspx .
Public Service Pension Plan (PSPP) - https://www.pspp.ca .
Management Employees Pension Plan (MEPP) - https://www.mepp.ca .
Alberta Public Service Benefit Information - https://www.alberta.ca/alberta-public-service-benefits .
Professional learning and development - https://www.alberta.ca/professional-development-support-directive .
Research Alberta Public Service Careers tool – https://researchapscareers.alberta.ca .
Positive workplace culture and work-life balance.
Leadership and mentorship programs.


Instructions For Your Resume

For Employment Experience:


Please indicate duration of employment (month, year).
Please specify employment status (i.e., casual, part-time or full-time).
Example – Research Analyst, January 2013 to June 2017, Part Time (three, 8 hour shifts/week)


For Post-Secondary Education


Please specify your major and length of program.
Please specify the year you graduated.
Example – Bachelor of Science, 4-year Degree (Graduated 2017).


How To Apply

Applicants are advised to provide information that clearly and concisely demonstrates how their qualifications meet the advertised requirements, including education, experience, and relevant examples of required competencies.

Candidates are required to apply for a job online. Please visit https://www.alberta.ca/job-application-resources#before for more information. Please visit Recruitment Principles , for more information.

In your resume, please include dates (including months and years) associated with all education and work experience. For example, January 2006 - June 2009 (part-time). This example is provided for illustrative purposes only. Applicants are encouraged to present their information clearly and thoroughly, using a format that best conveys their experience.

It is recommended applicants who have completed post-secondary studies from outside of Canada obtain an evaluation of their credentials from the International Qualifications Assessment Service (IQAS)( https://www.alberta.ca/international-qualifications-assessment.aspx ) or from a recognized Canadian Credential Evaluator; please visit the Alliance of Credential Evaluation Services of Canada for more information ( https://canalliance.org/en/default.html ).

It is recommended that applicants include the assessment certificate from IQAS or any other educational assessment service as part of their application.

Closing Statement

This competition may be used to fill future vacancies, across the Government of Alberta, at the same or lower classification level.

We thank all applicants for their interest. All applications will be reviewed to determine which candidates' qualifications most closely match the advertised requirements. Only individuals selected for interviews will be contacted.

If you require any further information on this job posting or require an accommodation during the recruitment process, please contact Krista Lammie at Krista.Lammie@gov.ab.ca .

Title: Business System Analyst
Description:
Role : Business Systems Analyst

Location: Toronto (Hybrid — 3 day/week )

Job type: Contract(6 Months) - High possibility of Extention

Pay : CAD $70 /Hour

Start date : Immediate

**Experience with Wealth Management and Capital Markets will be an asset***

Role Overview:

We are seeking a Business Systems Analyst with strong experience in Wealth Management and Capital Markets technology and operations. The successful candidate will act as a bridge between Wealth business stakeholders and technology teams, supporting the design, implementation, and optimization of advisor‑led Wealth platforms, investment workflows, and data integrations in a highly regulated environment.

Key Responsibilities:

Gather, analyze, and document business and system requirements for Wealth Management and Capital Markets platforms, including advisor tools, trading, and portfolio workflows
Partner with Wealth business teams (Advisors, Operations, Product) to identify process improvements and technology solutions
Translate business requirements into functional and technical specifications, including rules, controls, and exception handling
Work closely with Development, QA, and Business teams throughout the delivery lifecycle
Support system testing, implementation, and post‑launch stabilization activities
Collaborate with IT, vendors, and Risk/Compliance teams to ensure solutions meet regulatory, suitability, and governance requirements
Assist with data analysis, system integrations, and reporting related to Wealth products and investment data

Technical Skills:

Strong SQL skills for data analysis, validation, and reconciliation
Ability to analyze application code using AI tools to support impact analysis and solution understanding
Experience interpreting and troubleshooting XML request/response messages in system integrations and APIs
Familiarity with system integrations across trading, portfolio, and downstream platforms

Business Knowledge:

Strong understanding of Wealth Management and Capital Markets business models
Knowledge of investment vehicles, such as funds, equities, fixed income, and trades
Experience with trade lifecycle processes, reconciliations, and post‑trade operations
Familiarity with advisor workflows, client onboarding, and suitability considerations

Qualifications:

Proven experience as a Business Systems Analyst in banking or financial services, preferably supporting Wealth or Capital Markets domains
Experience working across business, development, and QA teams in a structured delivery environment
Strong analytical, documentation, and communication skills

Nice to Have:

Hands‑on experience with Wealth platform implementations or upgrades
Exposure to regulatory and compliance requirements impacting Wealth and Capital Markets
Experience supporting exception‑based, judgment‑driven investment workflows

Work Arrangement:

Hybrid model with on‑site presence required 2 days per week

Education:

Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Information Systems, or a related field (or equivalent experience)

Please share profiles with hr@hish.ca

Title: CI Data and Advancement Analyst
Description:
Job Information

Job Title: CI Data and Advancement Analyst

Job Requisition ID: 85041

Ministry: Children and Family Services

Location: Edmonton, AB

Full or Part-Time: Full-Time

Hours of Work: 36.25 hours per week

Permanent/Temporary: Temporary (12 months)

Scope: Open Competition

Closing Date: July 10, 2026

Classification: Program Services 3 (PS3)

Salary: $2,935.39 to $3,850.29 bi-weekly ($76,613.67 - $100,492.56/year)

The Ministry of Children and Family Services funds programs and services to support vulnerable children, youth, families, and individuals to live safely and succeed in Alberta. This includes providing services and resources to protect children and youth from abuse and neglect and effectively address harm that has placed them in need of intervention. Mentoring and support services are available to youth transitioning into healthy, productive adults.

The ministry also invests in community-based groups that support prevention and early intervention services such as Family Resource Networks, youth emergency shelters as well as programs that raise awareness and help respond to family violence, sexual violence, and abuse.

To find out more about our work visit: https://www.alberta.ca/children-and-family-services

Role Responsibilities

Are you passionate about using data to drive meaningful improvements in public services? The Ministry is seeking a CI Data and Advancement Analyst to transform program data into insights that support evidence-based decision making and service quality improvement.

Reporting to the Manager, CI Program Analysis and Performance, you will conduct advanced research and analysis, develop service quality indicators, and produce targeted reports that inform program strategy. You may also work with program policy teams, regional service delivery partners, Delegated First Nations Agencies, Information Management, and Human Resources to identify and report on trends and recommend improvements that strengthen service delivery.

As a key resource within the Branch and across the Ministry, you will also support cross-divisional initiatives, contribute to committees, and help ensure data and research inform continuous improvement efforts.

Key Responsibilities Include


Develop and deliver data reports, dashboards, and visualizations: Design, build, and maintain automated reports and dashboards using tools such as Excel, SQL Server Reporting Services (SSRS), and Power BI to support ministry reporting initiatives.
Conduct advanced data analysis and research: Analyze program data and external sources to identify trends, evaluate service quality indicators, and generate insights that support evidence-based decision making, program improvement, and strategic planning.
Manage data extraction, integration, and quality assurance: Extract, clean, validate, and integrate data from multiple internal and external sources while ensuring accuracy, reliability, and timely refreshes of reporting products.
Collaborate with internal stakeholders to support program performance: Collaborate with program areas, Information Management, IT, regional teams, and other ministry partners to clarify reporting requirements, enhance data systems, and develop performance measures and indicators.
Provide data-driven advice and issue management support: Proactively identify risks, reporting challenges, and emerging issues related to data quality, reporting timelines, and program performance, and provide recommendations to management.
Support knowledge sharing and effective communication of insights: Present complex technical information in clear, user-friendly formats, maintain documentation and data resources (e.g., data dictionaries, methodologies), and manage reporting platforms such as SharePoint to support information access and transparency.


Roles Responsibilities Cont'd

Drawing upon your superior data analysis, communication and presentation skills, you will build collaborative environments with various stakeholders and program areas to enhance best practices and continuous improvement in program delivery. Your proven critical thinking, analytical, and research skills will assist you with data extraction, refinement and report building.

Our ideal candidate must have a passion for data analytics, research, and report building. We are looking for a candidate who takes initiative in supporting complex projects and brings creative problem-solving and data presentation skills. Excellent written, verbal, and facilitation skills are essential for this role, along with the ability to effectively organize and manage multiple tasks, adapt to shifting priorities, and carry tasks to completion.

This role requires the ability to work both independently and collaboratively as part of a team to deliver projects within tight timelines.

Please click on this link to view the job description for this position.

Qualifications

University graduation in a related field (i.e. Science, Information Science, Social Science, Business Administration or Public Administration/Management, Mathematical and Statistical Sciences) along with 2 years progressively responsible related experience in conducting analysis, data reporting and applied research (quantitative and qualitative) is required; or equivalent as described below.

Preference may be given to candidates with experience using SQL to extract, manipulate, validate and analyze data from relational databases.

A master's degree in a related field is preferred.

Equivalencies

Directly related education or experience considered on the basis of:


1 year of education for 1 year of experience; or
1 year of experience for 1 year of education.


Assets


Proficiency with SQL and Report Builder, and experience using Power BI to pull data, perform data analysis, and build interactive reports.
Strong knowledge of research methodologies, statistics, and data analysis techniques.
Experience using data visualization and reporting tools (e.g., SSRS, Power BI, SAS VA) to translate complex data into clear insights.
Proven ability to clearly communicate technical or sensitive information and collaborate effectively with diverse stakeholders.
Understanding of Government processes and Child Intervention programs, including awareness of issues impacting Indigenous Peoples and communities.


Minimum recruitment standards outline the minimum education and experience required for appointment to a job classification.

Refer to https://www.alberta.ca/alberta-public-service-minimum-recruitment-standards .

APS Competencies

Competencies are behaviors that are essential to reach our goals in serving Albertans. We encourage you to have an in depth understanding of the competencies that are required for this opportunity and to be prepared to demonstrate them during the recruitment process.

This Link Will Assist You With Understanding Competencies

https://www.alberta.ca/system/files/custom_downloaded_images/psc-alberta-public-service-competency-model.pdf .


Agility - Demonstrates adaptability in a dynamic environment by proactively identifying opportunities to improve processes, adjusting to evolving priorities, and maintaining a calm, solution-focused approach while working creatively within established guidelines.
Creative Problem Solving - Uses analytical thinking and curiosity to understand complex data and reporting challenges, asking thoughtful questions, exploring new approaches, and leveraging diverse data sources to identify insights and improve reporting processes and program outcomes.
Systems Thinking - Applies a broad, strategic perspective to analyze data and program outcomes, considering interrelationships, emerging trends, and long-term impacts. Evaluates the implications of different options and identifies potential risks or unintended consequences to support informed decision-making and continuous improvement.


Notes

Temporary Position – This is a temporary position that is anticipated to be 12 months in duration.

This competition may be used to fill future vacancies across the Government of Alberta at the same or lower classification level.

Hours of Work: This position works Monday to Friday, 36.25-hours a week.

Location: Edmonton, Alberta

Application Requirements


Complete all pre-screening questions.
A cover letter outlining why you are applying to this role and how your experience relates to the qualifications is required.
In your resume, please include dates (including months and years) associated with all education and work experience. For example, January 2006 – June 2009 (part-time). This example is provided for illustrative purposes only. Applicants are encouraged to present their information clearly and thoroughly, using a format that best conveys their experience.
Candidates will require a clear Criminal Record Check and Intervention Record Check (if applicable) to be considered for the role (if not currently in the Child Intervention Delivery Services Branch).
Please be advised that your current supervisor and manager will be contacted as references.


The final candidate for this position will undergo appropriate pre-employment checks and provide suitable security screening.

Any costs associated with obtaining the required documents/checks as noted or interview travel expenses will be the responsibility of the candidate. Out-of-province applicants can obtain the required documents/checks from the province they currently reside in.

Note


Written Assessment may be used as part of the screening/selection process.

In your resume, please include dates (including months and years) associated with all education and work experience. For example, January 2006 - June 2009 (part-time). This example is provided for illustrative purposes only. Applicants are encouraged to present their information clearly and thoroughly, using a format that best conveys their experience.

It is recommended applicants who have completed post-secondary studies from outside of Canada obtain an evaluation of their credentials from the International Qualifications Assessment Service (IQAS)( https://www.alberta.ca/international-qualifications-assessment.aspx ) or from a recognized Canadian Credential Evaluator; please visit the Alliance of Credential Evaluation Services of Canada for more information ( https://canalliance.org/en/default.html ).

It is recommended that applicants include the assessment certificate from IQAS or any other educational assessment service as part of their application.

Closing Statement

This competition may be used to fill future vacancies, across the Government of Alberta, at the same or lower classification level.

We thank all applicants for their interest. All applications will be reviewed to determine which candidates' qualifications most closely match the advertised requirements. Only individuals selected for interviews will be contacted.

Title: Growth Associate
Description:
About Venn

Venn is building modern financial infrastructure for Canadian businesses. Built in Canada for Canadian entrepreneurs, Venn gives over 10,000 businesses access to the tools they need to manage money faster, easier, and more affordably.




We've raised an $21.5m Series A from Tier 1 investors, and we're rapidly growing and looking to hire A-players to change the future of banking in Canada.




Note: This role is 5 days a week in our Office located on Richmond and Spadina in downtown Toronto




What You'll Be Doing

Build the 0 → 1 product marketing and growth engine across channels: You’ll own Venn’s voice, narrative, and market presence across channels while building the systems we’ll scale from scratch, including product launch motions, brand messaging, campaign planning, tooling, channel strategy, and content distribution.
Own the narrative and messaging across Venn's product launches, website, content strategy, customer emails, and onboarding flows to improve acquisition and retention
Lead product launches and own the GTM plan for new products / features — narrative, positioning, assets, content, rollout, and tracking
Own and operate all social media channels, including LinkedIn, curating content and case studies for both the Venn brand and our founders to grow awareness and authority + discover new channels
Define and refine how Venn's products are perceived in the market, crafting compelling narratives that resonate with different ICPs.
Own the website and make it conversion-ready by constantly iterating to enhance messaging, drive conversions, and communicate product value effectively.
Create and execute a strategy for blogs, case studies, email campaigns, and educational content that drive conversions and engagement.




What we’re looking for:

Minimum 3+ years of full-time (non-internship) experience, including a minimum 2+ years at a tier-1 consulting firm, investment bank, or major technology company, plus at least 1 year of experience at a startup, as a founder, or as a creator/influencer
Strong understanding of product positioning, content distribution, engagement metrics, and audience growth.
Experience optimizing website content, SEO, and conversion rates.
Ability to analyze data, run experiments, and iterate based on insights.
Excellent copywriting, editing, and storytelling skills.
Strong cross-functional collaboration skills, especially with product and sales teams.
Ability to manage projects end-to-end, working with internal teams and contractors.
Self-starter and able to own and execute strategies end-to-end




This is not a new-grad role. Please only apply if you completed your undergraduate degree in 2023 or earlier.




Bonus Points

Experience at an early-stage or fast-growing startup
Experience at a fintech
Experience with video
Currently leveraging AI to automate / accelerate processes (e.g., Claude Code)




Perks & Benefits

Healthy equity ownership, among the most generous in the market, aligning your success with the company’s growth
Comprehensive coverage of medical, dental, and vision insurance
Opportunity to shape the future of business banking in Canada and beyond

Title: Business Analyst
Description:
Rates.ca Group Ltd. is growing!




We are looking for a highly self-motivated, talented and detail-oriented Business Analyst to join our Product team in Toronto. Reporting to the Director, Innovation and Digital Services, the Business Analyst position plays a key role in the insurance team comprised of full stack developers, quality assurance analysts, and product managers to implement best-in-class digital insurance products. The ideal candidate will have experience collaborating with stakeholders from various departments to solicit and manage product requirements.




Responsibilities of the Business Analyst will include but not be limited to:




Elicit, analyze, specify, and validate the business needs of stakeholders.
Work closely with the product team to determine project scope in accordance with the product vision and prioritize the backlog.
Use various requirements gathering methods to get all the information for a given requirement.
Identify and establish scope and parameters of requirements analysis on a project-by-project basis to define project impact, outcome criteria, and metrics.
Follow the requirement gathering processes in place and recommend improvements where needed.
Participate in the QA and UAT of implemented solutions to ensure features and functions have been enabled and optimized.
Analyze and verify requirements for completeness, consistency, comprehensibility, feasibility, and conformity to standards.
Utilize standard templates to accurately and concisely write requirements specifications, converting conceptual requirements into clear functional requirements understandable by everyone on the team.
Create process models, specifications, diagrams, and charts to provide direction to the scrum team.
Manage and track the status of requirements throughout the project lifecycle; enforce and redefine as necessary.
Communicate changes, enhancements, and modifications of business requirements verbally or through written documentation to scrum team, sponsors, and other stakeholders so that issues and solutions are understood.
Maintain documentation for all the products for as-is specifications to be referenced by the team.




Qualifications & Skills:




Ability to exercise independent judgment and act on it
Excellent listening, interpersonal, written, and oral communication skills
Logical and efficient, with keen attention to detail
Excellent analytical, mathematical, and creative problem-solving skills
Proficiency in SQL is preferred
Highly self-motivated and directed
Ability to effectively prioritize and execute tasks while under pressure
Experience working in a team-oriented, collaborative environment
Demonstrated project management skills and project management software skills, including planning, organizing, and managing resources




Education & Experience:




College diploma or university degree in the field of business administration, finance, or information systems
5+ years of work experience (Experience in P&C insurance industry is preferred)
Proven experience with business and technical requirements analysis, elicitation, modeling, verification, and methodology development




Check out the primary brands that we work on here: https://corporate.rates.ca/#brand-snippet-ratesdotca




Life @ Rates.ca is fun for everyone.




Our perks:

You get your Birthday off!
Work from home flexibility - Hybrid Model (2 office days per week - Tuesday and Thursday)
4 weeks of 'Work from Anywhere Program' every year
Full employer-paid benefits
RRSP Matching Program
Annual holiday festivities, free lunches and so much more
Helping you help others with our paid volunteer programs
Employee satisfaction survey for you to voice your opinion on working at Rates.ca Group Ltd.
Free access to LinkedIn Learning
Lounge and games room - ping-pong, pool, foosball, and more!




Rates.ca Group Ltd. and its subsidiaries are committed to being an Equal Opportunity Employer. We provide equal opportunity to all qualified individuals regardless of race, color, religion, age, gender, gender expression, national origin, veteran status, disability or any other legally protected categories such as sexual orientation. We believe that diversity, equity and inclusion are critical to our mission - creating a foundation for a creative workplace that leads to innovation and growth. We value diversity and strive to create an inclusive, accessible workplace where all individuals feel valued, respected, and heard.




We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to candidates and employees with physical or mental disabilities. If you require accommodation in order to search for a job opening or to complete any part of the application or hiring process, please send an email to work@rates.ca.

Title: Customer Care Technology & Analytics Coordinator
Description:
Location Address:

100 Queens Quay East, 9th Floor, Toronto

Number of Openings:

1

Pay:

$66,671.00 - $113,578

Job Posting Description:

This is an onsite role [].

Do you love the idea of helping shape the Customer Service experience through data? Reporting to the Customer Care Centre Manager, you will analyze large data sets to provide actionable insights to retail leadership. You will create periodic reports on performance metrics while identifying key trends, risks, and opportunities for improving the customer experience.

As the technical expert within the Customer Care team, you will maintain and administer customer experience (CX) systems, telephony systems, and ticketing systems. Your management of these systems allows our team to achieve our promise of delivering remarkable, seamless experiences for B2C and B2B clients.

If you are data centered with experience with collaboration and troubleshooting, then this is the role for you!

About The Role



Influence strategic decision-making by translating insights from complex data analysis into clear findings for a non-technical audience.
Translate statistics on Customer Care KPIs into simplified visualizations used to prompt action addressing key trends, risks, and opportunities.
Prepare customized daily reports (e.g. call volumes, response and wait times, customer satisfaction) to support ongoing business operations and performance tracking.
Prepare and deliver executive-level presentations that contextualize KPIs and allow key stakeholders to make data-driven decisions.
Perform advanced data mining techniques to uncover hidden patterns, correlations, and root causes impacting customer service performance.
Extract, clean, and analyze large, complex datasets from multiple sources to identify trends and actionable insights.
Maintain and administer customer experience (CX) systems, telephony systems, and ticketing systems.
Liaise with internal and external technical specialists to diagnose, troubleshoot, and resolve system and software issues, ensuring minimal disruption and continuity of business operations.
Identify gaps and inaccuracies in system data; recommend and implement system enhancements where applicable.
Participate in enterprise level projects aimed at improving effectiveness of servicing processes.



About You



Successful completion of related post-secondary education.
2+ years of experience working in data analytics, using MS PowerBI to create data visualizations, communicate complex analysis and support data-driven outcomes through KPI reporting.
Technical expertise with administering telephony systems (e.g. Computer Talk, Genesys), Ticketing/Chat Systems (e.g. ZenDesk, ServiceNow), and customer experience (CX) systems (e.g. Oracle Customer Experience).
2+ years of experience working in telecommunications with customer experience (CX) systems, telephony systems, and ticketing systems.
Experience explaining technical ideas to a non-technical audience in a way that is easy to understand.
Demonstrated proficiency with relational database management systems, including MS Access and Oracle, is essential to efficiently extract, manage, and analyze high volumes of customer interaction data.
Background in Python or similar scripting language and the ability to program with APIs.
Experience using advanced features of MS Excel, Access, and PowerPoint.
Formal training or certification in PowerBI or similar analytics software is an asset.
A highly developed understanding of Call Centre Operations or experience in a Customer Care Centre is an asset.



We offer a comprehensive suite of benefits including:



Health/Dental Benefits
Access to an Employee & Family Assistance Program
a Defined Benefit Pension
Discounts on products and services with LCBO program Workperks.

Title: Data Modeler & Data Engineer (Guidewire)
Description:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Data Modeler & Data Engineer (Guidewire) based in Canada.

This is an outstanding opportunity for a senior data professional to design and optimize enterprise-scale cloud data platforms supporting the Property & Casualty insurance industry. In this role, you will combine data architecture, engineering, and modeling expertise to build scalable Snowflake solutions that power analytics and business intelligence. You'll collaborate with cross-functional teams to modernize data ecosystems, improve performance, and ensure high standards for governance and security. Working in a fully remote environment, you will play a key role in transforming complex insurance data into reliable, business-ready assets. This position is ideal for someone who thrives at the intersection of cloud technologies, data engineering, and enterprise architecture.

Accountabilities


Design and develop conceptual, logical, and physical enterprise data models that support reporting, analytics, and operational business processes.
Build and optimize Snowflake data architectures, implementing scalable schemas, clustering strategies, materialized views, and performance tuning initiatives.
Translate complex business requirements into efficient data models using dimensional modeling techniques, including Star and Snowflake schemas.
Collaborate with data engineering teams to design and implement robust ELT/ETL pipelines and modern data lakehouse architectures using Medallion principles.
Model and integrate Guidewire data across PolicyCenter, ClaimCenter, BillingCenter, underwriting, policy administration, claims, billing, and customer domains.
Design cloud-native data ingestion frameworks leveraging AWS services while ensuring seamless integration across enterprise data platforms.
Establish and maintain enterprise data governance standards, including data quality, metadata management, lineage, security, compliance, and role-based access controls.
Partner with business stakeholders and technical teams to deliver scalable, secure, and high-performing data solutions that support strategic insurance initiatives.


Requirements


8+ years of experience in Data Architecture, Data Modeling, or Data Engineering within enterprise environments.
Strong expertise in conceptual, logical, physical, and dimensional data modeling, including Star Schema and Snowflake Schema design.
Extensive hands-on experience with Snowflake architecture, data modeling, query optimization, and performance tuning.
Advanced SQL development skills and strong programming experience with Python and PySpark.
Proven experience designing and supporting ELT/ETL pipelines and modern cloud-based data platforms.
Hands-on experience with AWS technologies, including S3, Glue, and Lambda.
Solid understanding of Medallion Architecture and modern data lakehouse concepts.
Deep knowledge of Guidewire data models, including PolicyCenter, ClaimCenter, and BillingCenter.
Strong Property & Casualty (P&C) insurance domain expertise, including policy administration, underwriting, claims management, and billing processes.
Excellent analytical, communication, collaboration, and problem-solving skills, with the ability to translate business requirements into scalable technical solutions.


Benefits


Permanent remote working model.
Flexible work arrangements based on employee and business needs.
Opportunity to work on large-scale cloud data transformation initiatives.
Exposure to cutting-edge technologies including Snowflake, AWS, and Guidewire platforms.
Collaborative and inclusive work environment.
Welcome Back Program supporting professionals returning after extended health or family leave.
Career growth opportunities within a rapidly expanding organization.
Diverse and inclusive culture that values innovation, collaboration, and professional development.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

  • Strong Property & Casualty (P&C) insurance domain expertise, including policy administration, underwriting, claims management, and billing processes.
  • Excellent analytical, communication, collaboration, and problem-solving skills, with the ability to translate business requirements into scalable technical solutions.

Benefits

  • Permanent remote working model.
  • Flexible work arrangements based on employee and business needs.
  • Opportunity to work on large-scale cloud data transformation initiatives.
  • Exposure to cutting-edge technologies including Snowflake, AWS, and Guidewire platforms.
  • Collaborative and inclusive work environment.
  • Welcome Back Program supporting professionals returning after extended health or family leave.
  • Career growth opportunities within a rapidly expanding organization.
  • Diverse and inclusive culture that values innovation, collaboration, and professional development.

How Jobgether Works

Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. Title: Sr. Data and Reporting Analyst
Description:
Why You’ll Love Softchoice

We are a software-focused IT solutions and services provider that equips organizations to be agile and innovative, and for their people to be engaged, connected, and creative at work. That means moving them to the cloud, helping them build the workplace of tomorrow, and enabling them to make smarter decisions about their technology. By doing these things we help them create success for their customers and their people.

We stand proudly for our people and support their success through career development and advancement. We are recognized and respected for our culture of inclusion and belonging, continuously striving to do what’s good for our people and communities.

The Impact You Will Have

The Sr. Data and Reporting Analyst will be responsible for owning the design, delivery, and maintenance of People and Culture (P&C) reporting and business analysis. This role serves as the subject-matter expert on workforce data—driving reporting strategy, ensuring data integrity across P&C systems, and providing consultative guidance to senior leaders that shapes how the organization makes people decisions. As a senior team member, you will partner cross-functionally with business analysts and operational leaders across Softchoice to combine people data with business performance data, turning insight into action. You will lead end-to-end analytics initiatives with minimal direction, influence without authority, and bring executive presence to every stakeholder interaction.

What You’ll Do


Develop and own reporting dashboards using data mining analytics and reporting tools (SuccessFactors Story, ADP Vantage, PowerBI, Power Platform) to drive performance monitoring and executive decision-making.
Design, develop, and maintain data and statistical models, including complex data manipulation from multiple sources that generate actionable insights for leadership.
Serve as the P&C analytics subject-matter expert, proactively advising senior leaders on key metrics, trends, and data-driven recommendations that inform workforce strategy
Conduct a review of the intake and prioritization of reporting requests and ensure reporting data is presented in an understandable and user-friendly format.
Engage stakeholders at all levels in defining requirements, developing technical specifications, analyzing data flow, and completing testing for report execution.
Conduct audits on data extracted to ensure the highest quality is being used for reporting.
Perform quality assurance checks on system configurations including assessment of changes in SAP bi-annual releases to determine the impact.
Use HR system and process expertise to analyze and troubleshoot issues, identify root causes, and determine and execute resolution.
Support the implementation and maintenance of data and reporting strategy and governance within P&C and throughout the partnership with business areas.
Champion the empowerment of stakeholders at various levels in leveraging reporting tools to support strategic and operational needs.
Respond to benchmarking surveys, and prepare analysis on employee data across P&C Systems
Serve as the SME for P&C analytics, by owning the timely generation of recurring metrics.
Ensure reporting roles and access are defined and maintained in partnership with IT governance and SuccessFactors Administrators
Create and maintain Knowledge-Based Documentation (KBDs) related to new developments and enhancements in reports and dashboards for knowledge sharing and job shadowing.
Onboard new HR Technology team members on P&C reporting tools, including training and change management.
Works with other HR Technology team members to build awareness of data and process changes impacting business metrics.
Maintains current knowledge of reporting trends and best practices for long-term solutions.



What You’ll Bring To The Table


Bachelor’s degree in business analytics, management analytics, math, statistics, computer science or related field
A minimum of 5 years’ experience working with Analytics tools at an advanced level with 2 years in PowerBI and Power Platform tools.
Experience with data visualization tools with at least 1 full implementation of Planning, Forecasting BI, and Predictive Analytics
Enrolled or completing Data Analytics certification or programs is an asset (Microsoft Certified: Power BI Data Analyst or People Analytics programs)
Demonstrated experience in using queries and syntax to conduct data mining, transformation, cleansing, and visualization.
Intermediate to Advanced experience with Microsoft Office products and SQL for querying relational databases.
A broad understanding of HR business fundamentals with at least 4 years of HR operations experience
Experience in 1-2 full-cycle SAP SuccessFactors implementation projects is an asset.
Experience with owning governance, controls, processes, data modeling, master data management, data standardization
Should be able to model positive behavior in the engagement with others and display the desired values and culture of the organization.
Demonstrated ability to drive initiatives end-to-end with minimal direction, taking full ownership from problem identification through solution delivery.
Works collaboratively to provide services that are sought after and recognized for being of high quality.
Executive presence with the ability to distill complex technical information into clear, actionable narratives for senior leadership and non-technical audiences.
Must be able to handle multiple tasks and projects with deadlines and adaptable to changes in priority level that require a high level of organization and planning skills.
Must be able to work quickly and efficiently without sacrificing the quality of their work.
Detail-oriented with proven analytical and problem-solving skills
Strong consultative skillset—able to frame technology issues, plans, and solutions from both a business and technical perspective, providing both pros and cons for recommended solutions to peers, people leaders, and senior leadership.
Excellent documentation skills (Process and training manuals, workbook updates)



Compensation

A reasonable estimate of the current base pay range for this position is $76,500 to $102,000 annually + 10% Target Incentives.

Actual salary will be based on a variety of factors, including location, experience, skill set, education, and related certification. The range for this position in other geographic locations may differ.

Softchoice offers a comprehensive and competitive benefit plan to all full-time employees, which includes:


Health and Wellbeing: Medical and Dental Care, Employee & Family Assistance Program
Financial Benefits: RRSP/DPSP Retirement Savings Plan with Company Matching, Life and Disability Insurance
Paid Time Off: Vacation and Sick Leave, Holidays, Parental Leave, Volunteer Days, Bereavement Leave
Additional Perks: Employee Discount Program



Not sure if you qualify? Think about applying anyway:

We understand that not everyone brings 100% of the skills and experience for the role.

At Softchoice, we offer opportunities to a diverse group including those with a variety of workplace experiences and backgrounds. Whether you are new to corporate tech, returning to work after a gap in employment, or looking to transition and take the next step in your career, we are excited to learn more about you and encourage you to apply.

Why You’ll Love Working Here


The People: You’ll thrive in our collaborative environment, surrounded by incredible colleagues who foster support and innovation, driving our collective success
High-Performing Culture: At Softchoice, we are dedicated to achieving our goals and committed to success for our customers and each other
Flexibility: Plan your workdays in a way that suits you best
Award-Winning Workplace: Proudly recognized as a Great Place to Work for 20 consecutive years
Inclusive Culture: We are committed to an inclusive culture where every team member can be their authentic self
Competitive Benefits: Benefit from competitive perks that start on day one



Inclusion & Equal Opportunity Employment

We are an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity, inclusion & belonging. People seeking employment at Softchoice are considered without regard to any protected category including but not limited to, race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex, marital status, ancestry, disability, veteran status, gender identity, or sexual orientation.

Require accommodation? We are ready to help:

We are proud to provide interview & employment accommodation during the recruitment and hiring process. If you require any accommodation to apply or interview for a position, please reach out directly to asktalentacquisition@softchoice.com . We are committed to working with you to best meet your needs.

Our Commitment To Your Experience

We are committed to the safety of all applicants and team members. With that in mind, we have implemented digital interviewing for everyone. We understand that you may need to interview with distractions around you (such as children or furry friends) and we will be doing the same.

Before you start with us, we will conduct a criminal record check, verify your education, and check your references.

When you join Softchoice, we will onboard you remotely. Don't worry. It's quick, simple and you'll be connected with your new team in no time.

Title: Consultant Business Intelligence
Description:
We’re building a relationship-oriented bank for the modern world. We need talented, passionate professionals who are dedicated to doing what’s right for our clients.

At CIBC, we embrace your strengths and your ambitions, so you are empowered at work. Our team members have what they need to make a meaningful impact and are truly valued for who they are and what they contribute.

To learn more about CIBC, please visit CIBC.com

What You'll Be Doing

As a member of CIBC’s Client Account Management, Business Intelligence team, you'll advance business intelligence by delivering actionable insights through data analysis. You'll leverage analytical techniques such as pattern recognition, data mining, and data storytelling to equip senior management with information for informed decision-making. Your responsibilities include providing data-driven insights, translating business requirements into effective dashboards, recommending improvements across key performance indicators, and conducting in-depth analysis to resolve data issues. You'll work independently to research analytics industry best practices, recommend enhancements and automation, and standardize data processes to drive strategic value for the department.

At CIBC we enable the work environment most optimal for you to thrive in your role. Details on your work arrangement (proportion of on-site and remote work) will be discussed at the time of your interview.

How You'll Succeed


Business Intelligence - Identify insights and make recommendations based on data analysis to enhance business decision-making.
Strategic Thinking Approach - Drive, manage, and execute data and reporting projects and initiatives in alignment with CIBC’s strategy, supporting business goals, objectives, and key performance indicators. Demonstrate the ability to think strategically, recognize early patterns in data enhancements and automation, and deliver strategic change across the enterprise.
Relationship Management - Manage relationships between Data & Reporting and various partners across the bank, with a strong focus on client experience and collaborative success. Build internal and external relationships as needed to ensure we provide the right level of service to our leadership team and business partners.
Communication - Possess excellent verbal and written communication skills to effectively articulate ideas and opinions, including those involving analysis, processes, and technology solutions to audiences with diverse backgrounds. Produce a variety of business presentations and documents that demonstrate command of language, clarity of thought, and orderliness of presentation.
Values, Integrity, and Trust - Demonstrate unwavering integrity and a commitment to ethical conduct in all interactions. Foster a culture of trust and accountability by consistently upholding CIBC’s values and acting with transparency and respect.
Reporting - Oversee the delivery of new reports and conduct thorough analysis to ensure data accuracy and timeliness. Support the tracking and communication of performance metrics. 


Who You Are


Risk or Collections Professional. You bring over 3 years of experience in credit risk or collections or possess a minimum of 5 years of business intelligence expertise within the banking sector or a leading financial institution.
Technical Proficiency. Skilled in programming languages such as SQL, PySpark, and Python, with hands-on experience using analytical tools including SAS, Databricks, Alteryx, as well as visualization and automation platforms like Power BI and Power Apps.
Problem Solver. You demonstrate exceptional critical thinking, problem-solving, and decision-making abilities, with a proven track record of independently identifying and analyzing business challenges and recommending effective solutions.
Certifications as an Asset. Certifications in Microsoft Power BI, Microsoft Power Automate, Databricks Developer for Apache Spark, or Databricks Data Engineer are considered valuable assets.
Outstanding Communication Skills. You excel at producing clear, concise business documents and can effectively communicate verbally, adapting your tone, style, and structure to suit your audience.
Values matter to you. You bring your real self to work and you live our values - trust, teamwork, and accountability.


What CIBC Offers

At CIBC, your goals are a priority. We start with your strengths and ambitions as an employee and strive to create opportunities to tap into your potential.


We work to recognize you in meaningful, personalized ways including a competitive compensation, a banking benefit*, wellbeing support and additional offers such as employee and family assistance programs and MomentMakers, our social, points-based recognition program.
Our spaces and technological toolkit will make it simple to bring together great minds to create innovative solutions that make a difference for our clients.
Subject to program terms and conditions


What You Need To Know


CIBC is committed to creating an inclusive environment where all team members and clients feel like they belong. We seek applicants with a wide range of abilities and we provide an accessible candidate experience. If you need accommodation, please contact Mailbox.careers-carrieres@cibc.com
CIBC is committed to clarity in our hiring process. All roles posted are opportunities we’re actively recruiting for, unless stated otherwise.
You need to be legally eligible to work at the location(s) specified above and, where applicable, must have a valid work or study permit
We may ask you to complete an attribute-based assessment and other skills test (such as simulation, coding, French proficiency).
We use artificial intelligence tools during the recruitment process. Our goal for the application process is to get to know more about you, all that you have to offer, and give you the opportunity to learn more about us.
