HTML:
As a Data Engineer on the R&D Team, you will help FreshBooks build and evolve high-quality, trusted data assets that power analytics, business decision-making, and machine learning initiatives. You will focus on data modeling, transformation, and domain-oriented data architecture, working closely with Product, Analytics, and Machine Learning teams to ensure data is well-structured, well-documented, and easy to consume. You will contribute to building scalable, reliable datasets that serve as a foundation for reporting, experimentation, and operational use cases, with exposure to both batch and event-driven data.

NOTE: This role can be worked remotely from the above location(s).

What You'll Do

  • Architect, design, and develop clean, high-performance datasets using modern tools like dbt and BigQuery, focusing on usability and scalability for analytical consumption.
  • Be a key contributor to our domain-oriented data architecture, defining how core business entities (e.g., customers, payments) are modeled, governed, and exposed across the organization.
  • Build and maintain robust batch and streaming data pipelines that transform raw data into trusted, analytics-ready assets to support both near real-time and traditional use cases.
  • Collaborate closely with Analytics, Product, and Machine Learning teams to translate complex requirements into reusable, well-governed data models and contracts.
  • Champion data quality, reliability, and documentation by implementing rigorous testing, validation, and monitoring practices.
  • Leverage cutting-edge tools, including AI/agentic workflows, to accelerate development, enhance productivity, and improve data exploration and lineage.
  • Participate in code reviews, contribute to improving engineering standards, and partner with platform teams to ensure our data solutions meet ambitious performance, cost, and scalability goals.

What You'll Bring

  • 2+ years of experience working in data engineering, analytics engineering, or a related field.
  • Experience building and maintaining data models and transformation pipelines (e.g., dbt or similar tools).
  • Strong SQL skills and proficiency in Python (or similar language).
  • Solid understanding of data modeling concepts (e.g., dimensional modeling, normalization, data warehousing patterns).
  • Experience working with a cloud data warehouse (e.g., BigQuery, Snowflake, Redshift).
  • Familiarity with orchestrators such as Airflow, GCC, Dagster, Prefect (or similar tools).
  • Basic understanding or exposure to streaming/event-driven systems (e.g., Pub/Sub, Kafka, Kinesis, Dataflow).
  • Understanding of data quality, testing, and validation practices.
  • Ability to work cross-functionally and communicate clearly with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

You'll Stand Out If You Have

  • Experience in analytics engineering or working closely with analytics teams.
  • Experience building or contributing to near real-time data pipelines.
  • Familiarity with data governance, metadata management, or lineage tools.
  • Experience using AI-assisted or agentic tools to improve development workflows.
  • Experience in SaaS, fintech, or payments-related domains.

Compensation

At FreshBooks, we approach each individual's compensation with a promise of fair market value and internal equity commensurate with experience and specific skill set.

The total compensation for this role ranges from CA$102,400 - CA$128,000 per year, which may include participation in our incentive programs. Additionally, this position offers an equity grant and immediate enrollment in FreshBooks' comprehensive benefits program, detailed below.

NOTE: This posting is reflective of an existing vacancy at FreshBooks.

Perks And Benefits

We understand that you'll weigh many factors when deciding if FreshBooks is the right next step in your career. Here are some of our offerings for our employees (we call ourselves ✨ FreshBookers✨) to support their growth:

  • 🩺 Comprehensive health and wellness benefits. Generous time off including a flexible vacation plan, a retirement savings program or pension plan matched to your local office, stock options for every full-time employee, parental leave and new parent support, annual healthy living credit, comprehensive medical and dental benefits, fertility and gender-affirming benefits dependent on your region, and more.
  • 📚 Perks that matter. Including a Peer Recognition Program, an Employee Assistance Program, headphone credit, meaningful in-person gatherings to bring onsite and remote employees together, and more perks matched to your local cluster.
  • 🌱 Working environments to help you thrive. Beautiful office spaces welcome you in Toronto, Amsterdam, and San Luis Potosi. If you don't have access to a FreshBooks office, you receive a home office credit to set up your home office.
  • 🤗 Supportive peer group, mentors, and leaders. We care about each other across the organization and have programs to support this so everyone feels a strong sense of belonging, and believes in collective impact.
  • 🚀 Accelerated growth. Comprehensive company onboarding, career development through continuous coaching, training, and learning on the job.

About FreshBooks

You've probably learned a bit about FreshBooks before making it to this point, but let us take a moment to introduce ourselves: At FreshBooks, we believe that small business owners are the backbone of the economy, yet they are often the most underserved when it comes to technology. For over 20 years, we’ve been on a mission to support owners, from the scrappy solopreneur with a big dream to the growing business with employees to manage.

Our leading platform is more than just business software; it’s an all-in-one financial toolkit that automates invoicing, expenses, payments and payroll, allowing our customers to spend less time on paperwork and more time doing the work they love.

We believe in human-centric connections that empower teams to do their best work. Headquartered in Toronto, Canada, our team operates in clusters across North America and Europe using a variety of working environments - remote, hybrid, and in-office - all based on what’s best for the team, collaboration, and mental well-being. We are committed to creating a supportive and flexible workplace where everyone can thrive, grow, and make an impact on the lives of small business owners worldwide.

Even if your experience doesn't meet every bullet on the above lists, we'd love to learn more about you and why you think FreshBooks is the next step in your career. FreshBooks is an equal-opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on gender, religion, race, mental disability, sexual orientation, age, or any other status. All applicants are considered based on their qualifications and merits. At FreshBooks, we inspire an environment of mutual respect and we believe diversity and inclusion are crucial to our success. Title: Quantitative Data Analyst, Investment Data
Description:
We are looking for a Quantitative Data Analyst to join our Quantitative Equity Team! We are a high-performance team embedded in a top-performing quant fund that manages over $75 billion in financial assets. We are dedicated to the mission-critical operation of our investment engine—a well-tuned machine responsible for generating key decisions that drive trades and investment insights. Leveraging data analytics, finance knowledge and cutting-edge technology, we aim to ensure that new and useful data products are continuously ready for research.

Do you love the idea of integrating large proprietary data sources into valuable applications? Are you a wizard at transforming ‘messy reality’ into high quality data assets? As a core member of our team, you will collaborate with investment and data experts to tackle challenging problems, and to continuously expand our capabilities in data. Based on the west coast in Vancouver, we value mentorship, collaboration, and growth in a supportive and innovative environment. Join us to make a significant impact on our investment outcomes and overall success.

What You Will Do

This is an exciting full-time role for individuals who are enthusiastic about learning the quantitative equity investment management business and excited to tackle a broad range of data, investment, and technology challenges.

You will start with a comprehensive training program, learning about various elements of quantitative equity investment management and our investment process.

You will then continue to a specialized role utilizing data analytics and data science skillsets to enable the Quantitative Equity Team’s research. Your role will involve identifying and assessing brand new data, data exploration, and proposing robust ways to integrate data products from many sources.

You will be supported with coaching and mentorship from senior members of the team, and we will create the conditions for you to grow your career steadily over time. This is an opportunity for ambitious individuals

to drive their career growth in data.

Role

While we take the approach of tailoring the career path to individual strengths, we expect successful candidates for this role to develop excellence in the following tasks:


Create processes to assess, explore, and link billions of data points.
Test structured and unstructured data sets for data quality and reliability.
Assess new data opportunities for quality and usefulness.
Evaluate your data solutions for sensibility, accuracy, and bias.
Collaborate with quantitative researchers – support alpha projects with your data expertise.
Collaborate with technology specialists – help trade off application performance and operational feasibility.
Support production deployments of your work to ensure long-term reliability.
Enable clarity of and access to your work through robust documentation and exposition.
Present findings and recommendations to key stakeholders in a clear, concise, and compelling manner.


What You Bring


Analytical Mindset – You think critically and creatively and are adept at balancing intuition with statistical validation.
Financial Knowledge – Desire to acquire the financial context needed to understand your stakeholders and make data decisions with impact.
Self-Starter – You are motivated and willing to take personal accountability for quality and timeliness of work.
Collaborative Approach – You thrive in a collaborative environment and value shared success. You proactively solicit and provide input and excel at communicating complex and technical concepts.
Time Management – You are able to manage and prioritize multiple ongoing projects.
Academic Training – At a minimum, an undergraduate degree in finance, mathematics, statistics, computer science, engineering, or related discipline. Completion of a relevant graduate degree is considered an asset.


We welcome applications from candidates with different levels of experience, including interns, new graduates, and experienced professionals. The base salary range for full-time roles is $125,000 – $200,000 annually plus a competitive performance bonus. Compensation will be determined based on experience and qualifications.

To apply for this position, please submit your resume, cover letter and academic transcripts.

CC&L Financial Group is committed to creating a diverse and inclusive environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to gender, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation or expression, disability, or age. 

Your application will be reviewed by a member of the hiring team - AI is not used in the screening, assessment or selection of applications at this time.

Title: IT Support Analyst
Description:
Lawson Lundell LLP


lawsonlundell.bamboohr.com


Published

June 18, 2026

Location

925 West Georgia Street, Vancouver, Canada

Category

Information Technology

Job Type

Full-time

Minimum

$63,000

Maximum

$68,000

Description

Lawson Lundell LLP is a leading regional Canadian business law firm with offices in Vancouver, Kelowna, Calgary and Yellowknife, who is known for its practical, strategic approach to legal and business problems. The firm is recognized and respected by its peers in legal and business publications and was recently named one of BC’s Top Employers for 2026. Business in Vancouver also recognized Lawson Lundell LLP as the fastest growing law firm in Western Canada.

We are currently recruiting for the position of IT Support Analyst (Help Desk). This role is located in our Vancouver office. Join a team of IT professionals that enjoy technology and support each other in the delivery of exceptional IT services to the firm.

Responsibilities for this position include, but are not limited to:

IT Support Responsibilities


Tier 1 IT Support – troubleshoot requests coming from calls and emails to the IT Help Desk.
Provide after hours on‐call support on rotating schedule.
Support & setup of Audio/Video conferencing technology in meeting rooms.
Support for computer equipment & office phone setup and moves.
Investigate, troubleshoot and resolve end‐user application or peripheral issues. This may involve working with application support teams and vendors.
Support our IT environment, including Virtual & Physical desktops, mobile device management (MDM), access & identity management (AD, Entra, MFA), Microsoft Office suite, document management, endpoint and web security, and supporting video/web conferencing platforms – Cisco Webex, MS Teams, and Zoom.


Qualifications


Certificate or diploma in information technology or computer systems
Microsoft Office/Windows experience
2+ years’ experience in supporting end‐user technologies, including Windows 11, Microsoft 365 apps/cloud services, Adobe, Mac/iOS, remote access (RDP, VPN), VOIP, A/V technologies
Strong technical and analytical skills
Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English
Good Interpersonal skills and a positive attitude
Ability to be resilient when dealing with stressful situations or people
Self‐motivated and able to multi‐task
Willing to work flexible hours when required
This position is required to be onsite in the downtown Vancouver
Successful completion of a Canadian criminal records check


Nice to have


Experience working in a laws firm or professional services organization
Microsoft Certifications
You have a background in standard IT technologies and a strong understanding of ITIL


The typical salary range for this position is $63,000 - $68,000 annually; the base pay offered is based on location and may vary depending on job-related knowledge, skills, experience, internal and market equity.

In addition to a competitive salary, we offer a variety of benefits, including:


Health Benefits: extended health, dental, vision, out of country, life, AD&D.
Lifestyle Benefit: reimbursement for purchases from a pre-approved list, including memberships and gym equipment.
Tenant Fitness Centre: access to the fitness centre in our office building
Paid Parental Leave top up.
Paid Time Off: vacation time starts at 3 weeks, personal days, sick time, and a volunteer day every year.
Education Reimbursement: we are committed to continued growth and development, and our educational reimbursement policy provides opportunity for our staff to expand on their skills and knowledge in the legal field.
Financial Benefits: defined contribution pension plan matched by the firm, RRSP, TFSA, year-end bonuses and an employee referral bonus program.
Fun Environment: annual year end party, happy hours, staff appreciation week, and many other social events.
Awards: long term service and bonus vacation awards.


If you are interested in becoming part of our dynamic environment and would like to apply for this position, please submit your resume with a covering letter to the attention of Kevin Bahng, Chief Information Officer (kbahng@lawsonlundell.com) or by clicking here.

Although we thank all applicants for their interest in Lawson Lundell LLP, only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

Title: Clinical Systems Analyst
Description:
Clinical Systems Analyst (AI Training)

About The Role

Your healthcare IT expertise is more valuable than ever — not just in hospitals, but in shaping the next generation of AI. At Alignerr, we partner with the world's leading AI research labs to build and train cutting-edge models. We're looking for Clinical Systems Analysts to help ensure AI understands the complex, high-stakes world of clinical technology.

This is a fully remote, flexible contract role where your real-world knowledge of EHRs, clinical workflows, and healthcare infrastructure directly improves how AI reasons about these systems.


Organization: Alignerr
Type: Hourly Contract
Location: Remote
Commitment: 10–40 hours/week


What You'll Do


Evaluate and provide expert feedback on AI-generated responses related to clinical systems, healthcare IT, and hospital workflows
Review technical scenarios involving EHR configurations, system troubleshooting, and clinical application support for accuracy and realism
Identify gaps, errors, or unsafe recommendations in AI outputs and suggest improvements
Translate your clinical and technical experience into structured, actionable feedback that helps AI models reason more accurately
Work independently and asynchronously on your own schedule


Who You Are


Experienced in supporting or administering healthcare IT systems — EHRs, clinical applications, or hospital infrastructure
Strong technical troubleshooting and system optimization skills
Able to bridge the gap between clinical workflows and technical implementation
Detail-oriented with clear written communication skills
Comfortable working independently on task-based assignments


Nice to Have


Prior experience with data annotation, data quality, or AI evaluation workflows
Background in EHR platforms such as Epic, Cerner, or Meditech
Experience in clinical informatics, health IT consulting, or systems integration


Why Join Us


Work on cutting-edge AI projects alongside top research labs and teams
Fully remote and flexible — set your own hours and workload
Freelance perks: autonomy, variety, and global collaboration
Contribute meaningful domain expertise to AI systems that impact healthcare
Potential for ongoing work and contract extension

Title: Data Migration Specialist
Description:
Our client is seeking multiple experienced ERP Data Migration Consultants to support a major Microsoft Dynamics 365 implementation. The project includes Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations and Dynamics 365 Customer Engagement, with a focus on migrating, validating, and reconciling business-critical data from legacy systems into the new ERP environment.




The successful consultants will work closely with business stakeholders, functional leads, technical teams, data owners, and implementation partners to support data migration planning, mapping, cleansing, transformation, testing, issue resolution, and cutover activities. This role requires strong ERP implementation experience, a practical understanding of business processes, and the ability to translate functional data requirements into accurate, complete, and usable migrated data.




Candidates with direct Microsoft Dynamics 365 experience will be strongly preferred. Candidates with SAP, Oracle, NetSuite, Workday, or other major ERP migration experience may also be considered where they bring strong functional module knowledge and hands-on data migration experience.




Key Responsibilities




Support end-to-end ERP data migration activities across planning, extraction, mapping, cleansing, transformation, validation, testing, reconciliation, and cutover.
Work with functional leads and business stakeholders to understand data requirements for Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations and Customer Engagement modules.
Analyze legacy data sources and define source-to-target mappings, transformation rules, data dependencies, and validation criteria.
Support data cleansing efforts by identifying data quality issues, duplicates, gaps, inconsistencies, and incomplete records.
Prepare, review, and maintain data migration templates, mapping documents, reconciliation reports, defect logs, and migration status updates.
Partner with technical teams responsible for ETL, integration, reporting, and data loading activities.
Participate in mock conversions, test cycles, system integration testing, user acceptance testing, and production cutover readiness activities.
Validate migrated data against business rules, functional requirements, and source system records.
Troubleshoot migration issues and work with business and technical teams to resolve defects.
Support data governance, auditability, security, and compliance requirements throughout the migration lifecycle.
Provide clear communication to project leadership regarding migration progress, risks, issues, dependencies, and readiness.
Contribute to cutover planning and post-migration stabilization activities.




Required Experience




5+ years of experience supporting ERP data migration, data conversion, data integration, or ERP implementation projects.
Hands-on experience with Microsoft Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations, Dynamics 365 Customer Engagement, Dynamics AX, or Dynamics CRM is strongly preferred.
Experience migrating data from legacy ERP, CRM, finance, supply chain, operations, sales, customer service, or master data systems.
Strong understanding of ERP data structures, business processes, and functional dependencies.
Experience with source-to-target mapping, data profiling, data cleansing, transformation rules, validation, reconciliation, and defect resolution.
Ability to work closely with both functional business users and technical data teams.
Strong Excel skills and familiarity with data migration tools, ETL processes, SQL, Azure Data Factory, KingswaySoft, SSIS, or similar tools.
Experience supporting multiple test migration cycles, mock loads, UAT, cutover, and go-live readiness.
Strong documentation, communication, analytical, and problem-solving skills.
For non-Dynamics candidates, strong functional module knowledge will be especially important. Candidates should be able to understand the business meaning of the data being migrated, not simply move data from one system to another.

Title: Data Security & DLP Analyst
Description:
Data Security & DLP Analyst (AI Training)

About The Role

At Alignerr, we partner with the world's leading AI research teams and labs to build and train cutting-edge AI models. We're looking for data security professionals who understand how sensitive information is exposed, mishandled, and protected in the real world.

This is a unique opportunity to apply your hands-on security expertise to a high-impact problem: teaching AI systems to reason accurately about data risk, DLP controls, and information security scenarios. Your real-world knowledge directly shapes how frontier AI understands and responds to data security challenges.


Organization: Alignerr
Type: Hourly Contract
Location: Remote
Commitment: 10–40 hours/week


What You'll Do


Analyze realistic data security and DLP scenarios spanning cloud, SaaS, and enterprise environments
Classify data sensitivity levels, exposure pathways, and policy violations
Evaluate prevention, detection, and incident response strategies for accuracy and completeness
Generate, label, and validate data security cases used to train and benchmark AI systems
Apply your practical understanding of how data risk plays out across modern organizations


Who You Are


2+ years of experience in data security, compliance, security operations, or a related field
Hands-on familiarity with DLP tools, data classification frameworks, and privacy or regulatory concepts (e.g., GDPR, HIPAA, CCPA)
Practical understanding of how sensitive data moves, leaks, and gets exposed in real environments
Strong written communication skills and ability to document findings clearly
Self-motivated and comfortable working independently on task-based assignments


Nice to Have


Experience with cloud security (AWS, Azure, GCP) or SaaS security posture management
Background in security policy development, risk assessment, or data governance
Familiarity with frameworks like NIST, ISO 27001, or CIS Controls
Prior exposure to AI, machine learning, or data annotation workflows


Why Join Us


Work directly on frontier AI systems alongside world-class research teams
Fully remote and flexible — work on your own schedule
Freelance perks: autonomy, variety, and global collaboration
Contribute to meaningful work that makes AI safer and more security-aware
Potential for ongoing work and contract extension

Title: Sr. Data and Reporting Analyst
Description:
Why You’ll Love Softchoice

We are a software-focused IT solutions and services provider that equips organizations to be agile and innovative, and for their people to be engaged, connected, and creative at work. That means moving them to the cloud, helping them build the workplace of tomorrow, and enabling them to make smarter decisions about their technology. By doing these things we help them create success for their customers and their people.

We stand proudly for our people and support their success through career development and advancement. We are recognized and respected for our culture of inclusion and belonging, continuously striving to do what’s good for our people and communities.

The Impact You Will Have

The Sr. Data and Reporting Analyst will be responsible for owning the design, delivery, and maintenance of People and Culture (P&C) reporting and business analysis. This role serves as the subject-matter expert on workforce data—driving reporting strategy, ensuring data integrity across P&C systems, and providing consultative guidance to senior leaders that shapes how the organization makes people decisions. As a senior team member, you will partner cross-functionally with business analysts and operational leaders across Softchoice to combine people data with business performance data, turning insight into action. You will lead end-to-end analytics initiatives with minimal direction, influence without authority, and bring executive presence to every stakeholder interaction.

What You’ll Do


Develop and own reporting dashboards using data mining analytics and reporting tools (SuccessFactors Story, ADP Vantage, PowerBI, Power Platform) to drive performance monitoring and executive decision-making.
Design, develop, and maintain data and statistical models, including complex data manipulation from multiple sources that generate actionable insights for leadership.
Serve as the P&C analytics subject-matter expert, proactively advising senior leaders on key metrics, trends, and data-driven recommendations that inform workforce strategy
Conduct a review of the intake and prioritization of reporting requests and ensure reporting data is presented in an understandable and user-friendly format.
Engage stakeholders at all levels in defining requirements, developing technical specifications, analyzing data flow, and completing testing for report execution.
Conduct audits on data extracted to ensure the highest quality is being used for reporting.
Perform quality assurance checks on system configurations including assessment of changes in SAP bi-annual releases to determine the impact.
Use HR system and process expertise to analyze and troubleshoot issues, identify root causes, and determine and execute resolution.
Support the implementation and maintenance of data and reporting strategy and governance within P&C and throughout the partnership with business areas.
Champion the empowerment of stakeholders at various levels in leveraging reporting tools to support strategic and operational needs.
Respond to benchmarking surveys, and prepare analysis on employee data across P&C Systems
Serve as the SME for P&C analytics, by owning the timely generation of recurring metrics.
Ensure reporting roles and access are defined and maintained in partnership with IT governance and SuccessFactors Administrators
Create and maintain Knowledge-Based Documentation (KBDs) related to new developments and enhancements in reports and dashboards for knowledge sharing and job shadowing.
Onboard new HR Technology team members on P&C reporting tools, including training and change management.
Works with other HR Technology team members to build awareness of data and process changes impacting business metrics.
Maintains current knowledge of reporting trends and best practices for long-term solutions.



What You’ll Bring To The Table


Bachelor’s degree in business analytics, management analytics, math, statistics, computer science or related field
A minimum of 5 years’ experience working with Analytics tools at an advanced level with 2 years in PowerBI and Power Platform tools.
Experience with data visualization tools with at least 1 full implementation of Planning, Forecasting BI, and Predictive Analytics
Enrolled or completing Data Analytics certification or programs is an asset (Microsoft Certified: Power BI Data Analyst or People Analytics programs)
Demonstrated experience in using queries and syntax to conduct data mining, transformation, cleansing, and visualization.
Intermediate to Advanced experience with Microsoft Office products and SQL for querying relational databases.
A broad understanding of HR business fundamentals with at least 4 years of HR operations experience
Experience in 1-2 full-cycle SAP SuccessFactors implementation projects is an asset.
Experience with owning governance, controls, processes, data modeling, master data management, data standardization
Should be able to model positive behavior in the engagement with others and display the desired values and culture of the organization.
Demonstrated ability to drive initiatives end-to-end with minimal direction, taking full ownership from problem identification through solution delivery.
Works collaboratively to provide services that are sought after and recognized for being of high quality.
Executive presence with the ability to distill complex technical information into clear, actionable narratives for senior leadership and non-technical audiences.
Must be able to handle multiple tasks and projects with deadlines and adaptable to changes in priority level that require a high level of organization and planning skills.
Must be able to work quickly and efficiently without sacrificing the quality of their work.
Detail-oriented with proven analytical and problem-solving skills
Strong consultative skillset—able to frame technology issues, plans, and solutions from both a business and technical perspective, providing both pros and cons for recommended solutions to peers, people leaders, and senior leadership.
Excellent documentation skills (Process and training manuals, workbook updates)



Compensation

A reasonable estimate of the current base pay range for this position is $76,500 to $102,000 annually + 10% Target Incentives.

Actual salary will be based on a variety of factors, including location, experience, skill set, education, and related certification. The range for this position in other geographic locations may differ.

Softchoice offers a comprehensive and competitive benefit plan to all full-time employees, which includes:


Health and Wellbeing: Medical and Dental Care, Employee & Family Assistance Program
Financial Benefits: RRSP/DPSP Retirement Savings Plan with Company Matching, Life and Disability Insurance
Paid Time Off: Vacation and Sick Leave, Holidays, Parental Leave, Volunteer Days, Bereavement Leave
Additional Perks: Employee Discount Program



Not sure if you qualify? Think about applying anyway:

We understand that not everyone brings 100% of the skills and experience for the role.

At Softchoice, we offer opportunities to a diverse group including those with a variety of workplace experiences and backgrounds. Whether you are new to corporate tech, returning to work after a gap in employment, or looking to transition and take the next step in your career, we are excited to learn more about you and encourage you to apply.

Why You’ll Love Working Here


The People: You’ll thrive in our collaborative environment, surrounded by incredible colleagues who foster support and innovation, driving our collective success
High-Performing Culture: At Softchoice, we are dedicated to achieving our goals and committed to success for our customers and each other
Flexibility: Plan your workdays in a way that suits you best
Award-Winning Workplace: Proudly recognized as a Great Place to Work for 20 consecutive years
Inclusive Culture: We are committed to an inclusive culture where every team member can be their authentic self
Competitive Benefits: Benefit from competitive perks that start on day one



Inclusion & Equal Opportunity Employment

We are an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity, inclusion & belonging. People seeking employment at Softchoice are considered without regard to any protected category including but not limited to, race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex, marital status, ancestry, disability, veteran status, gender identity, or sexual orientation.

Require accommodation? We are ready to help:

We are proud to provide interview & employment accommodation during the recruitment and hiring process. If you require any accommodation to apply or interview for a position, please reach out directly to asktalentacquisition@softchoice.com . We are committed to working with you to best meet your needs.

Our Commitment To Your Experience

We are committed to the safety of all applicants and team members. With that in mind, we have implemented digital interviewing for everyone. We understand that you may need to interview with distractions around you (such as children or furry friends) and we will be doing the same.

Before you start with us, we will conduct a criminal record check, verify your education, and check your references.

When you join Softchoice, we will onboard you remotely. Don't worry. It's quick, simple and you'll be connected with your new team in no time.

Title: Digital Analyst
Description:
Overview

Insight Global is hiring a Lead Digital Analyst to support a high-impact Product Analytics team focused on ecommerce web and mobile experiences. This is a hands-on individual contributor role for someone who operates like a lead—owning discovery, challenging stakeholders, and delivering insights that directly influence product and business decisions.

This role is ideal for someone curious, highly communicative, and able to deeply understand business objectives to design the right analytical approach—not just execute requests.

What You’ll Do

Own analytics initiatives end-to-end as a lead individual contributor
Lead stakeholder discovery, intake requests, and define measurement approaches aligned to business impact
Build deep understanding of ecommerce product goals and stakeholder objectives
Challenge and push back on stakeholders to ensure the right problems are being solved
Combine and analyze data from multiple sources using SQL and Python
Define KPIs, build measurement plans, and deliver actionable insights
Perform advanced analytics including regression, time series analysis, segmentation, A/B testing, and forecasting
Build automated reporting and scalable data pipelines
Present insights and recommendations to stakeholders, including senior leadership
Operate in a fast-paced, agile environment with shifting priorities

Must-Haves

4+ years of experience in digital analytics, ideally ecommerce or retail
Expert SQL and Python (hands-on)
Strong experience with Snowflake and Databricks
Experience working with ecommerce or digital product data
Strong foundation in applied statistics (regression, time series)
Structured, critical thinking approach to problem-solving
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills
Highly responsive, proactive, and comfortable working independently

Nice to Have

Power BI (building dashboards, not just consuming)
Experience with Google Analytics (GA4) or similar platforms
Retail or consumer product experience
Experience with data modeling or machine learning

Title: Functional Analyst
Description:
Engineers and Geoscientists British Columbia is currently searching for a Functional Analyst to join our team in Burnaby!

The Functional Analyst at EGBC plays a crucial role in enhancing off the shelf systems such as Microsoft Teams, SharePoint and Office365, to support existing and improved business processes. They recommend and configure off the shelf solutions to meet the needs of EGBC and system users. This involves conducting thorough assessments of current systems, gathering and documenting business requirements, and collaborating with key personnel to ensure alignment with EGBC's goals. They analyze systems, identify areas for improvement, and develop plans to implement new configurations, installations, or updates of existing systems that can benefit the organization. The Functional Analyst also provides training and support to end-users, ensuring they can effectively utilize new systems. By bridging the gap between business needs and technology solutions, the Functional Analyst ensures efficient and effective operations, ultimately driving business success and innovation.

Our organization - one of BC's Top Employers – offers a competitive salary, comprehensive and generous benefits, an inclusive culture, and a hybrid work environment. Full details on our compensation and benefits value offering can be found below.

What You Will Be Doing


Administering interviews, workshops, and surveys with business users, managers, and other audiences to gather detailed business requirements.
Developing detailed process maps to visualize current workflows and identify areas for improvement.
Analyzing existing off the shelf systems and business processes to identify inefficiencies, gaps, and areas for improvement.
Preparing IS and system users for upcoming feature and functional changes.
Ensure the compliance and security of solutions with relevant regulations such as FIPPA and incorporates robust security measures.
Designing technical specifications and system designs based on business requirements, including the creation of prototypes and mock-ups to visualize proposed solutions and gather feedback from audiences.
Collaborating with IS Infrastructure and Development teams to design system architecture that will support the functional requirements of a solution and complement existing IS services. Identifying and defining integration points with other systems and ensuring seamless data flow.
Overseeing and engaging in the implementation of new systems or updates, ensuring they are completed on time and within budget.
Conducting quality assurance testing, including unit testing, integration testing, and user acceptance testing (UAT).
Creating detailed documentation, including user manuals, system specifications, and training materials.
Scripting to perform data extraction, transformation, analysis and automate repetitive tasks and integrate various third-party software systems to enhance efficiency and streamline processes.
Training employees on new systems and processes through sessions and workshops. Developing training materials and conducting training sessions for end-users to ensure they can effectively use the new system.
Collaborate with the Project Management Office to move projects forward and follow project management and change management recommended practices.
Other duties as assigned by direct leader and, as appropriate, members of the divisional leadership team.


What You Bring To The Table

The successful applicant will possess the following:


5+ years of relevant previous experience in detailing, documenting and implementing technology solutions.
Bachelor's degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or related is preferred.
Certifications like PMI-PBA (Professional in Business Analysis) or CBAP (Certified Business Analysis Professional) can be advantageous.
3+ years of experience assisting clients with implementing software applications.
3+ years of experience in leading and managing projects, particularly those involving software development, system implementation, or process improvement.
3+ years of experience with software development life cycles, scripting, debugging, and data analysis.
Experience working in a support help desk or software support capacity.


What's In It For You

Each member of our team contributes to public safety in BC and helps us deliver on our vision of "modern regulation for a resilient world." We serve the public as an inclusive, progressive, and future-focused regulator. Our Strategic Plan also places priority on our role to advance climate action, foster equity, diversity and inclusion, and move towards reconciliation with Indigenous peoples. At Engineers and Geoscientists BC, we are committed to demonstrating our values of collaboration, excellence, integrity, and innovation.

This role offers a competitive compensation and benefits program including a salary within our hiring range of $86,500 to $96,500. Placement within the hiring range will be based on candidate experience relative to the requirements of the role. Additional details on our generous benefit offerings are found below.

Who We Are

Engineers and Geoscientists BC is proud to be recognized as one of BC's Top Employers.

Engineers and Geoscientists British Columbia is the business name of the Association of Professional Engineers and Geoscientists of the Province of British Columbia. We regulate and govern these professions under the authority of the Professional Governance Act.

We are charged with protecting the public interest by setting and maintaining high academic, experience, and professional practice standards for over 40,000 registrants. Individuals licensed by Engineers and Geoscientists BC are the only persons permitted by law to undertake and assume responsibility for engineering and geoscience projects in BC.

We are a not-for-profit organization governed by a board of elected registrants, licensees, and government appointees. The board is accountable to the public through the Ministry of Post-Secondary Education and Future Skills under the Office of the Superintendent of Professional Governance, for both the governance and management of the organization.

To learn more about our work please visit us at https://www.egbc.ca.

Engineers and Geoscientists British Columbia is the business name of the Association of Professional Engineers and Geoscientists of the Province of British Columbia. We regulate and govern these professions under the authority of the Professional Governance Act.

We are charged with protecting the public interest by setting and maintaining high academic, experience, and professional practice standards for over 40,000 registrants. Individuals licensed by Engineers and Geoscientists BC are the only persons permitted by law to undertake and assume responsibility for engineering and geoscience projects in BC.

We are a not-for-profit organization governed by a board of elected registrants, licensees, and government appointees. The board is accountable to the public through the Ministry of Post-Secondary Education and Future Skills under the Office of the Superintendent of Professional Governance, for both the governance and management of the organization.

To learn more about our work please visit us at https://www.egbc.ca.

Title: Market Analyst
Description:
Job Description

Onni is seeking a highly skilled and analytical Equity Market Analyst to join our dynamic team. The successful candidate will play a pivotal role in evaluating publicly/privately traded companies, with a strong focus on the mining sector. This position requires at least 5 years of proven experience in equity research, investment analysis, or a related field.

Perks & Benefits


Competitive pay & a Comprehensive Benefits Package.
Benefits include company-paid extended health care, dental care, vision care, life insurance, critical illness, and critical life insurance.
Annual Education Allowance
Generous Referral Program
Fun & collaborative company culture with lots of team building events
Local discounts to restaurants, clothing stores, personal training, and more
Free access to a fully equipped gym
Friends and Family rates for hospitality brands.


What You Will Do


Conduct detailed financial analysis and valuations of equities, particularly within the resource and mining industries.
Build and maintain financial models, including DCF, comparables, and scenario analysis.
Monitor and interpret relevant macroeconomic, industry, and company-specific developments that impact investment opportunities.
Generate investment ideas supported by thorough research, and communicate these to the broader investment team through written reports and presentations.
Track key performance indicators (KPIs), commodity price trends, and regulatory changes affecting mining and related companies.
Collaborate with portfolio managers and other analysts to develop sector-specific strategies and identify potential investment risks.


What You Will Bring


Minimum 5 years of experience as an equity research analyst, buy-side/sell-side analyst, or a similar role in investment banking or asset management.
Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, Accounting, or a related discipline (Master’s or CFA preferred).
Deep sector knowledge of commodities, mining operations, and resource-based economies is highly desirable.
Demonstrated track record of actionable investment insights and analytical rigor.
Strong preference for candidates with experience analyzing or working within the mining or natural resources sector.
Solid understanding of global financial markets, with a strong grasp of equity valuation techniques.
Proficiency in financial modeling and analytical tools (Excel, Bloomberg, Capital IQ, etc.).
Exceptional written and verbal communication skills.
Ability to work independently while contributing to a collaborative team environment.
CFA designation or progress toward it is an asset.


About The Company

For over half a century, Onni has been building communities for people to live, work, and play. Our success reflects our commitment to our employees and partners, and our dedication to quality construction, innovation, sustainability, and customer satisfaction. Our expertise expands across North America, with offices in Vancouver, Toronto, Los Angeles, Seattle, Phoenix, and Chicago.

We thank all applicants for their interest; however, only those selected for an interview will be contacted. No phone calls, please.

Salary Range

$120,000 - $250,000

About The Company

Onni

For over half a century, Onni has been building communities for people to live, work, and play. Our success reflects our commitment to our employees and partners, and our dedication to quality construction, innovation, sustainability, and customer satisfaction. Our expertise expands across North America, with offices in Vancouver, Toronto, Los Angeles, Seattle, Phoenix, and Chicago.

AI Use

This role may involve the use of artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support research, analysis, content development, design, reporting, or operational efficiency. Employees are expected to use AI responsibly and in compliance with company policies, data privacy requirements, confidentiality obligations, and applicable laws.

All AI-generated or AI-assisted outputs must be reviewed for accuracy, quality, and appropriateness before use. Employees are also expected to disclose when AI tools have been used in the creation of work and must not present AI-generated content as solely their own original work.

How To Apply

Please apply through the link on the job posting and attach your resume and any other required documents.

We thank all applicants for your interest in the Onni Group. Note that only those applicants under consideration will be contacted.

Title: Data Quality Assurance Analyst - FTT
Description:
Marketing Statement

A career at TransLink and our family of companies means working with people with a wide range of skills and perspectives, all teaming up towards a common goal: preserving and enhancing the region's world-envied quality of life. Together, we connect the region and enhance its livability by providing a sustainable transit and transportation network, embraced by our communities and people.

At TransLink we are dedicated to building a workforce that reflects the diversity of the communities in which we live. We’re committed to fostering an inclusive, equitable and accessible workplace, recognizing the unique value and skills every individual brings.

Looking for a great place to work where your contributions are valued and you can make a difference in a vibrant city? At TransLink, one of BC’s Top Employers, you'll help make Metro Vancouver a better place to live, built on transportation excellence. Put your future in motion!

Summary

Responsibilities

The Data Quality Assurance (QA) Analyst is responsible for ensuring the accuracy, consistency, and reliability of TransLink’s enterprise data assets and analytical systems. This role focuses heavily on data validation, ETL testing, and database quality assurance, supporting the delivery of trusted, high-quality data for business intelligence, analytics, and operational decision-making.

The Analyst creates and executes data-centric testing strategies, develops automated data validation frameworks, and partners closely with Data Architects, Solution Architects, Data Analysts, Data Warehouse Analysts, Functional Analysts, Business Intelligence Analysts, and other roles to ensure that data pipelines, integrations, and applications perform accurately and consistently across systems.

Education

Qualifications

Diploma in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Computer Systems Engineering, or Mathematics from a recognized post-secondary institution, including recent coursework in database management, data warehousing, cloud computing, and software testing is preferred.

Experience

Minimum three (3) years of experience in data testing, data validation, or ETL/warehouse QA within large-scale or cloud data environments including proven experience in testing data pipelines, ETL processes, and cloud-based data integrations, advanced proficiency in SQL and Microsoft SQL Server.

Experience validating Azure Data Factory or Azure Synapse Analytics workflows, background in test automation and scripting using Python or equivalent languages and familiarity with data observability, data profiling, and data governance frameworks preferred.

Up to one (1) year of on-the-job experience may be required to gain familiarity with TransLink’s specific systems and practices.

Other Information

Recruitment Process: An applicant will be required to demonstrate their suitability for this position by meeting the minimum level of qualifications and experience in order to be invited into the selection process. A standard interview format will be used including general, scenario and behavioural descriptive interview questions.

Work Schedule

37.5 hours per week.

This is a full-time temporary position for 12 months.

Work Designation

Hybrid

This position offers the flexibility of working both on-site and remotely within B.C.

Rate of Pay

Salary $8,236 - $9,896 per month (Actual salary offered will be commensurate with education, experience and internal parity).

The Total Compensation Package includes Extended Health, Transit Pass and enrollment in the Public Service Pension Plan upon eligibility. Focus on your development through training, and mentorship programs. Enjoy a variety of health and wellness programs, including access to gym facilities. Speak to us to know more about what we offer.

How To Apply

Please click the 'Apply' button at the top right corner of the page or go to http://www.translink.ca/careers to apply for this position and view instructions on the process.

INSTRUCTIONS: Please save your (1) cover letter, and your (2) resume as one pdf document prior to uploading your application on-line.

Posting Date: April 15, 2026

Closing Date:Open until filled

Please note that only those short listed will be contacted.

Having trouble applying? Please view the System Requirements & FAQ's by going to http://www.translink.ca/careers.

Equal Employment Opportunity

TransLink is committed to employment equity and building a diverse workforce, representative of the customers we serve and the many communities in the Metro Vancouver region. We welcome and encourage Indigenous applicants, people of colour, all genders, 2SLGBTQ+ and persons with disabilities to apply. Learn more about TransLink's commitment to equity, diversity and inclusion.

Accommodations are available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process. For a confidential inquiry, simply email us at jobs@translink.ca.

Title: Biotech Health Data Governance Lead
Description:
Biotech Health Data Governance Lead (AI Training)

About The Role

What if your expertise in biotech data governance could directly shape how AI understands and works with clinical research data — influencing the future of life sciences and scientific discovery?

We're looking for a Biotech Health Data Governance Lead to ensure that research and clinical trial data used in AI training and evaluation is accurate, traceable, compliant, and scientifically trustworthy. Your work will sit at the intersection of cutting-edge AI development and regulated life sciences research — a rare and meaningful place to operate.

This is a fully remote, flexible contract role built for experienced professionals who want autonomy, meaningful impact, and exposure to frontier AI and biotech research.


Organization: Alignerr
Type: Hourly Contract
Location: Remote
Commitment: 10–40 hours/week


What You'll Do


Govern biotech research and clinical trial datasets to ensure accuracy, lineage, and auditability for scientific analysis and regulatory submissions
Define and enforce data policies covering classification, access controls, security protocols, and metadata standards across research, clinical, regulatory, and partner teams
Enable secure, governed data access for analytics, AI model development, and external collaborations — while protecting confidential and patient-related information
Evaluate and improve data quality workflows, annotation standards, and data readiness for AI training pipelines
Collaborate across scientific, IT, compliance, and business stakeholders to align data governance frameworks with operational and regulatory requirements
Support the development of scalable, auditable data governance practices suited to fast-moving life sciences and AI research environments


Who You Are


Experienced in leading or implementing data governance programs within biotech, life sciences, clinical research, or other regulated data environments
Deeply knowledgeable about data privacy, security, compliance, and regulatory expectations for research and clinical trial data
Comfortable bridging scientific, technical, and business teams — translating complex governance requirements into clear, actionable standards
Detail-oriented and systematic — you build trust through thoroughness, consistency, and clear documentation
Self-directed and reliable when working independently in a remote, asynchronous environment


Nice to Have


Prior experience with data annotation, data quality assurance, or AI data evaluation systems
Familiarity with regulatory frameworks such as FDA 21 CFR Part 11, GCP, HIPAA, or ICH guidelines
Background in clinical data management, bioinformatics, or health informatics
Experience working alongside AI or machine learning teams in a data governance capacity


Why Join Us


Work at the frontier of AI and life sciences — contributing to projects that matter for both fields
Fully remote and flexible — structure your work around your schedule
Freelance autonomy with the substance of high-stakes, technically rigorous work
Gain exposure to advanced AI models and how high-quality, governed data drives better science
Potential for ongoing work and contract extension as new projects launch

Title: Client Services Analyst
Description:
About us




Aquanow is a trading and technology company powering the next generation of financial services. We’re at the forefront of the rapidly evolving digital asset space, empowering businesses to navigate the complexities of cryptocurrencies and blockchain technologies. As a leading provider of infrastructure solutions, we offer innovative services in cryptocurrency trading, liquidity management, and secure digital asset storage. Our platform is built to deliver seamless, scalable, and reliable solutions that cater to the needs of institutional clients and businesses seeking to unlock the potential of digital finance. Aquanow is committed to driving growth and shaping the future of the digital economy by offering unparalleled opportunities for innovation and advancement in the world of digital assets.




With rapid global expansion, we have established offices in Vancouver, Toronto, Dubai, Malaysia, Turkey, and beyond. Our international customer base includes some of the world’s fastest-growing fintech companies and leading financial institutions.




Your Contribution to Our Growth




We are looking for a motivated Client Services Analyst to join our Vancouver office and work within our Client Services Desk. This team is the front line support for our institutional clients, ensuring timely and accurate service for our traded products. This role is more complex than a typical support position, combining client-facing responsibilities with analytical, reporting, and operational work.




You will gain an in-depth understanding of how a financial services firm operates, support critical business functions, and help identify opportunities to streamline processes. This includes responding to client inquiries, assisting with product, payment, and transaction-related questions, managing settlements, and preparing reports. The role is well-suited for someone with strong communication skills, exceptional attention to detail, and a genuine interest in learning how a fast-paced fintech business operates.




This is a full-time, permanent opportunity, hybrid out of our Downtown, Vancouver office.




The successful candidate will complete training on a Monday to Friday schedule during their first few months. Upon completion of training, the role will transition to a regular Saturday to Wednesday schedule, including weekend and statutory holiday coverage as assigned.




What You’ll Do




Serve as the first point of contact for institutional client inquiries via chat and email.
Respond to client queries in a timely manner with professionalism and in accordance with company guidelines.
Guide clients on troubleshooting, provide product information, and resolve common platform and setup issues.
Manage and record client requests through our internal ticketing tool, ensuring tasks are routed or escalated as needed.
Take ownership of client-related tasks from initial request to completion.
Support settlement processes by handling client payments (on-chain and banking systems) accurately and on time.
Prepare internal and client-facing reports.
Ensure documentation is accurate and up to date.
Collaborate with counterparts in other time zones to ensure seamless 24/7 desk coverage.
Participate in ad-hoc projects and tasks as required.




We’d Love to See




Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Economics, Accounting or a related field is preferred.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills with a client-first mindset
1-3+ years of experience working in a similar field, such as finance, asset management, or banking
Strong attention to detail and comfort working with numbers.
Analytical skills and the ability to think through problems logically.
Proficiency in Microsoft Office (especially Excel); VBA or other basic programming skills are an asset.
Ability to prioritize and manage multiple tasks in a fast-paced, sometimes unstructured environment.
Positive attitude, eagerness to learn, and ability to work both independently and as part of a team.
Interest in the financial, fintech, digital asset domain.




At our offices you will find a group of people that embrace diversity and value performance over pedigree. Every day we draw value from the diverse experiences of our team whose backgrounds include top financial institutions, tech companies and untraditional backgrounds. Our success comes from identifying exceptional people and providing them with the platform to reach their true potential.




Aquanow is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We are committed to providing equal employment opportunity to all qualified persons without regard to race, color, creed, religion, sex, age, national origin, citizenship status, disability, qualified veteran status, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity or any other unlawful criterion or circumstance.

Title: Data Annotation Specialist, Data Science
Description:
Who are we?

Cohere is the leading security-first enterprise AI company. We build cutting-edge foundation AI models and end-to-end products that are designed to solve real-world business problems.

We’re training and deploying frontier models for enterprises who are building AI systems. We believe that our work is instrumental to the widespread adoption of AI and we are looking for folks that want to be part of that.

We obsess over what we build. Each one of us is responsible for contributing to increasing the capabilities of our models and the value they drive for our customers. Cohere is a team of researchers, engineers, designers, and more, who are all passionate about their craft.

We are a global technology company co-headquartered in Toronto and San Francisco, with key offices in London, New York City, Montreal, Seoul, Germany and Paris. Join us!

Why this role?

This role will focus on evaluating data science and coding tasks, requiring you to review and debug code, analyze model trajectories, and assess data visualization script development and logical flow implementation. Your work will contribute to our model development efforts and the logic our models apply when completing task requests.

Please note: This is a part-time independent contractor position available within Canada. We seek candidates who are able to commit to 16 hours per week minimum at a 40 CAD/hour contract rate. This role is BYOD 💻 - Bring Your Own Device (laptop). Remote work within Canada. 12 month contract. Performance incentives included!

As a Data Annotation Specialist, You Will


Evaluate the model's ability to respond to coding requests, workflows, and code base-related questions using available tools.
Assess agent trajectories and model capabilities for code generation, tabular and graphic manipulation, and debugging requests.
Prompt models to complete complex data science tasks and review the accuracy of generated responses.
Label, proofread, and improve machine-written and human-written software engineering-related outputs.
Report quality and performance trends related to model/agent behaviour and project assignments.


You May Be a Good Fit If You Have


Exceptional data analysis and visualization skills.
3+ years of relevant industry experience working on real-world data science problems and pipelines.
Proficient knowledge and understanding of Python and industry-standard data science packages (numpy, pandas, matplotlib, sqlite, or others).
Strong understanding of SQL syntax writing and workflows; and a deep familiarity with file/data formats, such as markdown, JSON, XML, YAML, and HTML.
Prior experience re-writing, proofreading, and delivering feedback on code.
Familiarity with code agents (OpenCode, Claude Code, Codex) or evaluating agent trajectories is a plus.


The Candidate Journey

Initial Screening - Once you have submitted your application, our Talent Team will review your resume and writing samples.

Virtual Annotation Test - This assignment will test your written and technical skills through various language-based tasks, such as a data science take-home assessment, writing sample, and more.

Video Screen - If selected to move forward, you will have a short video call with a member of our Operations Team!

Offer - Independent Contractor Agreement

As an independent contractor, you maintain control over how you complete your work and may work with multiple clients simultaneously. We request that you declare any external work relationships with Cohere’s direct competitors and always maintain the IP confidentiality of the Cohere project. Independent contractors are not eligible for health benefits or other benefits provided to employees. Compensation for services is provided to contractors by self-invoicing for services provided pursuant to the terms of our agreement with the contractor.

It is important to understand that, as an independent contractor, continuous work is not guaranteed. The client-contractor relationship is fundamentally project-based, meaning engagements may be temporary, periodic, or intermittent based on our organizational needs and project availability. As an independent contractor, you should anticipate fluctuations in workflow and, therefore, compensation for services when Cohere does not require as many hours of services in a week.

Prospective candidates, please be advised: this role involves working with human-generated and model-generated tasks that may involve exposure to not safe for work (NSFW) text content as part of data annotation tasks, including explicit, offensive, or other inappropriate material.

If the above qualifications do not perfectly align with your experience, we still encourage you to apply!

We value and celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive work environment for all. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to providing equal opportunities. Should you require any accommodations during the recruitment process, please submit an Accommodations Request Form, and we will work together to meet your needs.

Title: Business Systems Support Analyst
Description:
Business System Support Analyst

Vancouver BC(Hybrid)

Job Summary

Join a global mining technology leader where you'll play a pivotal role in improving the business systems that power operations worldwide, while partnering with stakeholders across the business to drive meaningful change and continuous improvement.

Key Selling Points


Work across enterprise systems including NetSuite, CRM and Business Intelligence platforms in a global technology environment
Influence business process improvement and digital transformation initiatives with direct exposure to senior stakeholders
Grow your career within a global organisation backed by Weir, supporting mining operations around the world


About Us

We are the world’s leading mining software business bringing together the expertise of Micromine, Motion Metrics and Fast2Mine.

Our solutions help mining companies explore, design, plan and operate more effectively through industry-leading software, AI, machine vision and automation technologies. From geology and mine planning to fleet performance and operational optimization, we help customers improve productivity, efficiency and sustainability across the mining value chain.

Backed by Weir's global reach and resources, we combine deep mining expertise with a strong innovation roadmap, delivering trusted technology solutions used by mining operations around the world.

The Opportunity

As our Business System Support Analyst, you'll become a trusted partner in enhancing the systems and processes that support a growing global business. This role offers the opportunity to combine technical problem-solving, business analysis and stakeholder engagement while contributing to projects that improve operational efficiency, user experience and business performance across multiple regions.

This will be part of Micromine.

The Impact you'll have


Help shape the effectiveness of critical business systems used across a global organisation.
Partner with stakeholders to identify opportunities that improve processes and business outcomes
Influence user adoption and system optimization through effective support and training
Contribute to business transformation initiatives that drive operational excellence
Build strong relationships across technical and non-technical teams globally
Develop expertise across enterprise applications while expanding your business analysis capability


What You Need To Succeed


3+ years of NetSuite or similar ERP systems supporting enterprise business systems
Strong business process analysis, process mapping and documentation skills
Proven ability to troubleshoot issues and translate technical concepts for diverse audiences
3+ years' experience in technical support, business systems support or a similar role
Experience gathering requirements and collaborating with stakeholders to improve business outcomes
A proactive, customer-focused mindset with strong communication and problem-solving capabilities


What You Can Expect From Us


A base salary range of CAD $90,000 to 100,000 CAD DoE
Extended health benefits, including allowances for family and wellness expenses
4 weeks paid Vacation and 2 weeks paid Personal Leave (i.e. Sick / Carer's Leave)
RRSP matching
Subsidized gym membership
Hybrid working arrangements to balance work and home life
Opportunities for professional development and progression
A team first culture and the chance to work within the mining technology industry


Apply Now

Are you ready to influence the systems, processes and user experience that support a global technology business? If you're passionate about solving complex problems, driving continuous improvement and creating meaningful business impact, we'd love to hear from you.

To learn more, visit our website at www.micromine.com or LinkedIn Life Page.

Weir Software Solutions is dedicated to fostering a diverse and inclusive work environment, and we welcome applicants from a variety of backgrounds to apply.

#Hybrid

#Micromine

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Reporting to the Manager, Business Intelligence & Analytics, the Senior Data Analyst will have a deep understanding of Data, AI, and Analytics solutions. You will be responsible for designing, developing, and delivering effective storytelling solutions using Power BI and Bird’s modern analytics platform.

We are seeking an individual who is proactive, curious, and driven—someone who understands not just how to build dashboards, but how to enable a culture of data analytics.

This role is ideal for an individual with a strong sense of the big picture: how data governance, storytelling, tools, and people come together to enable better decisions. You will play a key role in advancing Bird’s analytics maturity by delivering trusted insights, promoting effective data storytelling, and contributing to the Data & AI Center of Excellence (CoE) through standards, enablement, and data fluency.

The ideal candidate is comfortable navigating complexity, managing multiple priorities, and working across both Agile and Waterfall delivery models—partnering effectively with business, engineering, and leadership teams.

This position follows a hybrid work model with regular in office presence.

What You Will Be Working On


Design, develop, and deliver high‑quality analytics solutions using Power BI, ensuring accuracy, reliability, and consistency of data and metrics.
Apply effective data storytelling principles to transform complex information into clear, compelling, and decision‑ready insights.
Partner with stakeholders to understand business context, ask the right questions, and translate analytical needs into meaningful dashboards and reports.
Contribute to the evolution of the Data & AI Center of Excellence (CoE), helping shape standards, best practices, templates, and shared ways of working.
Promote data governance concepts by reinforcing trusted metrics, clear definitions, consistent logic, and proper documentation.
Support and advance data fluency and literacy across the organization by helping stakeholders better interpret, question, and use analytics effectively.
Perform analytical investigations and root‑cause analysis to explain trends, variances, and anomalies in business performance.
Collaborate with Data Engineering and IT teams to ensure analytics solutions align with data Lakehouse architecture and enterprise platforms.
Demonstrate a strong understanding of Data Lakehouse concepts, with exposure to tools such as Databricks, Microsoft Fabric, or Microsoft Synapse Analytics, sufficient to collaborate effectively with engineering teams.
Navigate and contribute within both Agile and Waterfall project environments, adapting communication and delivery style as needed.
Manage multiple priorities simultaneously while maintaining quality, structure, and stakeholder confidence.
Maintain strong, proactive communication with stakeholders and contribute to successful project outcomes.
Stay current with analytics trends and Microsoft’s analytics ecosystem (Power BI, Lakehouse platforms, and related technologies), applying learnings pragmatically.



What We Are Looking For


Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Business Analytics, or a related field.
5+ years of experience in a Senior Data Analyst or Business Intelligence role.
Strong hands‑on experience with Power BI, with an emphasis on insight, clarity, and usability rather than just visual complexity.
A proactive, self‑driven mindset — comfortable taking ownership, identifying gaps, and helping move analytics forward.
Demonstrated understanding of what building a data‑driven culture actually means, beyond tools alone.
Experience contributing to or working within a Center of Excellence–style model for Data, AI, Analytics, or BI.
Solid grasp of data governance fundamentals, including trusted metrics, standard definitions, and consistency of logic.
Strong communication and organizational skills, with the ability to work effectively across both technical and non‑technical audiences.
Experience working in both Agile and Waterfall delivery environments.
Ability to manage competing priorities with structure, judgment, and professionalism.
General understanding of the Microsoft Power Platform (Power Apps / Power Automate) without requiring deep specialization.



Preferred Qualifications


Experience delivering insights from structured, semi‑structured, and unstructured data sources.
Working knowledge of SQL and structured data querying.
Familiarity with modern data lakehouse architectures and exposure to platforms such as Databricks, Microsoft Fabric, or Synapse Analytics.
Power BI or relevant Microsoft analytics certification.
Experience enabling or mentoring others through data education, documentation, or informal



For Those Who Seek To Redefine

The greatest achievements in history are borne from the greatness within people – where human potential meets vision, and passion fuels evolution. Unlocking this potential is the most important thing we do at Bird.

As a leader in Canadian construction for over 100 years, the impact of our team is etched deeply within the core of our legacy. Beyond Bird, this impact is felt in the fundamental aspects of our everyday lives. From the critical infrastructure we depend on, to the energy and resources that keep us moving - we are powering our communities and shaping Canada’s skylines coast-to-coast-to-coast. Entrenched in the foundation of a culture built more than a century ago is an enduring quest to reimagine what is possible. Our impact is greater than ever, and we are looking for those who seek to redefine their story.

What We Believe In

We Put Safety First

A healthy and safe work environment is non-negotiable. We build a culture of operational and psychological safety through engagement, learning and leadership.

We Lead With Honesty

We speak and act with integrity, clarity and care so people can trust our word and our work. Being honest means we can deliver the best outcomes and consistent results.

We Are Stronger Together

Success is a team effort. Our inclusive workplace enables our combined expertise, humility and creativity to unlock our greater potential.

We Are Driven To Do Great Work

We built our name on quality. We have a passion for excellence in our work and relationships that honours our businesses and our industry.

We Create Opportunity

Rooted in a solid foundation, we adapt and grow to face the future. We are committed to elevating each other to chart the best path forward in an evolving world.

At Bird, we value Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DE&I) and believe it is essential to our success. We will continue to listen, learn, and take action in our commitment to building progress in our organization, and the industry as a whole. Cultivating an environment where all employees feel included, seen, and have a sense of belonging is core to Bird’s culture. We commit to proactively employing a workforce that reflects the communities in which we work, fostering an environment of continuous learning, and never compromising on our values.
